photos.caption: One man Bridge game for prisoners (cc @hoverbird)
photos.captionKii: I was really captivated by this vintage 'Autobridge' game set I saw. It's a 'Play-Yourself Bridge Game,' designed for solo play, which I found fascinating. The green box announced 'play bridge - learn alone - by actual play,' and the detailed gray game board had spaces for 'West Bids' and 'South Bids,' allowing one person to play a real four-handed bridge game against 'invisible experts.' It was labeled as an 'Advanced Set,' and there was even a note about 'Latest Bidding Changes.' It's such a unique glimpse into how people used to engage with card games and learn new skills, a truly cool piec

I found this really striking historical sign, commemorating the 'Alcatraz-Indians' occupation from November 1969 to June 1971. The illustration in the center, depicting what looks like a Native American figure with long dark hair and a peace sign necklace, is so powerful. It also mentions Richard Oakes as the 'first' leader and Yvonne Oakes as the 'first' 'casualty,' which hints at the significant and poignant history behind this movement. The bright, almost ethereal background with the water suggests the island setting. It was a really impactful piece of history to come across during my visit

photos.captionKii: I was absolutely fascinated by this incredible display dedicated to 'The Godfather.' The centerpiece for me was that gorgeous, antique gold rotary telephone, it just screamed classic movie prop. Behind it, the wall was covered with amazing pieces of history: I could make out a newspaper headline about 'Vito Corleone feared murdered,' the iconic 'The Godfather' novel by Mario Puzo, what looked like a production script, and a striking Newsweek magazine cover featuring Marlon Brando. There were also other stills from the film, really immersing you in the world of the Corleone family. It was such

photos.caption: Chilling by the ocean with a fire pit with my mom and sis.
photos.captionKii: I remember this incredibly relaxing moment, propped up with someone else's feet next to a cozy fire pit. My feet, with their bright red painted toenails in black sandals, were nestled right beside another pair of feet wearing a stylish leopard-print boat shoe and jeans. The fire was flickering warmly, casting a beautiful glow, and in the background, everything was a bit misty, suggesting we were somewhere with a cool, foggy atmosphere, maybe near the coast. I could see a wooden railing and a rustic-looking house in the distance, framed by lush green trees. It was such a peaceful and comfortabl

photos.caption: Hula hipster gas station grizzly
photos.captionKii: I couldn't help but smile when I spotted this fantastic bear statue at what looked like a gas station or a small town stop. This bear definitely had a unique style! It was decked out in a cool straw hat, complete with wild, messy straw hair, and sported these vibrant orange sunglasses that really made it stand out. Around its neck, it wore a fun lei of orange and red flowers, giving it a real vacation vibe. In the background, I could see some charming older buildings and a black pickup truck, making the whole scene feel like a classic American roadside discovery. It was such an amusing and une

photos.caption: Hey rock, next year, it's on. cc @tatagatha @bkeyink @meghantosh
photos.captionKii: I remember capturing this absolutely breathtaking view of Half Dome at what felt like a magical twilight hour. The sky was painted in the most incredible shades of soft purple and pink, creating such a serene and dreamlike atmosphere over the mountains. Half Dome itself stood out majestically, its iconic shape instantly recognizable, and I could clearly see the rugged textures of the granite rock face and the dark patches of resilient pine trees clinging to its slopes. It was a moment of profound beauty and tranquility, truly a highlight of my time in the park.

photos.caption: USGS. 7214 feet.
photos.captionKii: I remember spotting this really cool U.S. Geological Survey marker embedded in the ground, likely at a summit or significant point. It's a bronze disk with a clear warning about a 'FINE FOR DISTURBING THIS MARK,' which I found a bit amusing and also respectful of its historical value. The marker specifies an 'ELEVATION ABOVE SEA' of '7214 FEET' and the letters 'VA.', which tells me I was somewhere in Virginia at a considerable height. It was fascinating to see the original date of the survey, '7~11~1905,' making it over a century old. Finding these historical markers always adds an extra layer

photos.caption: Cool story, nature. (Epic Cliff remix)
photos.captionKii: I remember capturing this incredibly dramatic shot, focusing on the sheer, dark rock face that just dominated the frame. It looked so imposing and rugged, with subtle textures and cracks running through it, and I could even spot small tufts of resilient dry grass clinging to the steep incline. In the distance, through the bright, hazy light, I could make out more of the mountainous terrain and the dense green forest below, emphasizing the vast scale of the landscape around me. It truly felt like standing next to a giant wall of nature.

photos.caption: Cool story, nature. (Vernal Fall Rainbow remix)
photos.captionKii: I remember capturing this powerful waterfall, which was just breathtaking. The water was absolutely gushing down the dark, jagged rock face, creating a huge misty spray at the bottom. What made it truly magical was seeing that faint rainbow arching right through the mist where the sunlight hit the water, adding such a vibrant touch to the dramatic scene. It was incredible to witness nature's raw power and beauty all at once.

I remember coming across this rustic metal sign while exploring Yosemite, which was so helpful for hikers. It clearly listed some of the incredible destinations we could reach, like Nevada Fall, Glacier Point, and the famous Half Dome, along with their distances. Seeing 'Half Dome' listed really brought home how central it is to the park's identity, and it was exciting to see how far some of the trails stretched out to places like Clouds Rest, Merced Lake, and Tenaya Lake. It felt like a quintessential park experience, seeing these markers guiding our way through the beautiful wilderness.

photos.caption: Cool story, nature.
photos.captionKii: I remember taking in this absolutely breathtaking view, which I believe captures the iconic Half Dome rising majestically above the valley floor. The sky was such a brilliant, clear blue, perfectly contrasting with the rugged granite cliffs that stretched upwards. In the foreground, the dark silhouette of the pine forest created a beautiful sense of depth and scale, especially as the sunlight dramatically highlighted the peaks. It was an awe-inspiring moment, really showcasing the incredible grandeur of nature.

photos.caption: So this is what your engine looks like after Burning Man
photos.captionKii: I remember taking this close-up shot of my car's engine bay. You can clearly see the Volkswagen (VW) logo on the engine cover, which also indicates it's a 2.0-liter engine. What really stands out is how dusty everything is, which definitely suggests it was time for some cleaning or perhaps a check-up. It's not the most glamorous photo, but it's a practical reminder of keeping things running smoothly under the hood.

photos.caption: Indie video arcade #xoxofest
photos.captionKii: I remember walking through one of the dimly lit spaces at the XOXO Festival and spotting these really cool illuminated cubes. One in the foreground had a '1' projected onto its side, along with some pixelated imagery, while the cube further back had a distinct, blocky pattern that immediately made me think of Minecraft. It felt like such a perfect blend of technology and art, fitting right in with the festival's vibe, and I loved how they glowed in the dark, creating an interesting visual experience.

I remember seeing this striking 'EAST END' logo, presented in such a cool, stylized font. It was emblazoned within a dark circle, surrounded by those elegant, swirling art deco-like flourishes. The whole design had a really polished, almost metallic look, contrasting with what might have been a glass surface or a slightly worn wall. I think this was a sign for one of the specific areas or venues we visited during the XOXO Festival, marking another interesting spot as we explored all the different happenings there.

photos.caption: @ekai rocks the world
photos.captionKii: I remember seeing this guy, looking so cool in his bright turquoise sunglasses, enjoying a refreshing drink from a mason jar with a striped straw. It felt like such a relaxed moment amidst the buzz of the XOXO Festival, probably at one of the outdoor social gatherings or parties. He had a lanyard around his neck, just like many of us, which was a clear sign we were all part of the festival experience. The cityscape in the background also reminds me of the unique urban setting of some of the festival's venues.

photos.caption: omg, you guys, it's the nest. (!) #xoxofest — widen + kennedy
photos.captionKii: I remember being really captivated by this striking art installation, a massive, swirling form made of countless intertwined branches or twigs, almost like a giant, fantastical bird's nest. It was beautifully integrated into the wooden architecture of the space, possibly within one of the venues at the XOXO Festival. The way the light caught certain parts of the branches, giving them a warm, reddish glow, made the whole piece feel alive and dynamic. It was another interesting artistic discovery from the festival, adding to the rich mix of experiences there.

photos.caption: MBU in a mason jar. Where is @meghantosh and @emmettjez ??
photos.captionKii: I remember holding this refreshing drink in a mason jar, complete with cucumber slices, at what felt like a lively outdoor gathering. The late afternoon or early evening light was really beautiful, casting a warm glow over everything. I could see a crowd of people mingling in the background, enjoying themselves on what looked like a rooftop or patio. There were even some fun orange balloons dotting the distant skyline, adding to the festive atmosphere. It was such a nice moment, just relaxing and taking in the scene.

photos.caption: Opening talk @xoxo. Hooray humanity, awesomeness, creativity and, of course, internet hugs.
photos.captionKii: I remember being at this really engaging presentation, probably at the XOXO Festival, given the prominent branding on the big screen and the stage setup. The speaker was standing in front of the large white screen displaying 'XOXO' in a clean, modern font, and there was a cool, illuminated 'XOXO' sign on the right side of the stage too. The room was packed with people, all seated and looking towards the stage, creating such a great atmosphere of collective interest and learning. I loved soaking in the energy of the crowd and listening to the talk.

photos.caption: So glorified!
photos.captionKii: I remember this moment, standing with friends at the entrance of 'Peg's Glorified Ham & Eggs.' I loved the quirky sign on the floor mat, with the chef character and the promise of a great meal. You can see all of our feet gathered around, some of us in flip-flops, one in bright red sneakers, all ready to dig into some delicious breakfast or brunch. It was a fun way to start the day, getting ready to enjoy some food together.

photos.caption: Reno Reno Reno
photos.captionKii: I remember taking this shot, fascinated by the striking geometric patterns on the side of a tall building. The facade was made up of alternating vertical strips of a light, possibly concrete or stone material, and reflective glass windows that glowed with a warm, reddish-orange hue, perhaps reflecting the sunset or interior lights. Looking up, the strong lines created a really cool, almost abstract composition, capturing a unique aspect of urban architecture.

photos.caption: It never ends! — Post-Playa after party at the Grand Sierra
photos.captionKii: I remember this lively pool day, where the sun was shining brightly over a really crowded pool! There were so many people enjoying themselves, swimming and lounging around. I loved the clear blue water and the backdrop of those rolling hills or mountains in the distance, which made the whole scene feel so open and refreshing. It was a perfect way to spend the day, soaking up the sun and being around so much energy.

photos.caption: Playa makes the best hair. Also, sleep?
photos.captionKii: I remember this intimate moment, capturing a woman's portrait bathed in the unique, warm glow inside what felt like a tent or temporary structure in the desert. Her short, reddish-brown hair framed her face as she looked directly at the camera, wearing a striped tank top and a distinct round pendant. The soft, reddish-orange lighting created a truly atmospheric and cozy feeling, quite different from the expansive outdoor shots, highlighting the diverse experiences within the desert city.

photos.caption: 7 days of Playa hair IS SWEET
photos.captionKii: I remember catching this fun selfie of a woman winking in the bright desert sun! Her short, spiky brown hair was full of personality, and she had a vibrant pink bandana around her neck, which really stood out. She was also wearing a cool light-colored, sleeveless top with a perforated pattern, perfect for the warm weather. In the background, I could see the vast, dusty expanse of the desert, dotted with various vehicles and temporary structures, which truly captured the unique, adventurous atmosphere of our exploration.

photos.caption: SpaceCats / Hammock Hangout tear down
photos.captionKii: I remember this vibrant moment in the desert city, where a woman, possibly a friend or someone I met, stood proudly with arms outstretched, holding two blue banners. One banner clearly read 'COMMUNITY,' which truly captured the spirit of the place. She was wearing a cute white polka-dot top, a black skirt, and a pink bandana, looking ready for the day's adventures. In the background, you can see the expansive desert landscape with a tall red and white tower, tents, and other unique structures, all under the clear blue sky. The tire tracks in the dusty ground and her long shadow really emphasiz

photos.caption: ….nerds....
photos.captionKii: I remember being utterly mesmerized by this massive, glowing blue sphere, an incredible art installation rising high above the crowds. It looked like it was mounted on some kind of art car or structure, with people gathered on a platform below, all bathed in its intense, electric blue light. The intricate geometric lines of the sphere against the utterly dark night sky were truly spectacular, making it feel like we were observing a cosmic event or a miniature planet. It was such a vibrant and energetic sight, a testament to the incredible creativity found everywhere in the desert city at night

I remember encountering this absolutely stunning illuminated flower art installation at night. It was so captivating how the neon-like lights outlined each petal, creating such a vibrant glow against the dark desert sky. The main structure featured these large, delicate white flowers branching out, and below, there was another smaller bloom with a beautiful pink-purple hue, adding to the magical glow. Seeing people gathered around, admiring it, really added to the enchanting atmosphere of the event, making it feel like walking through a dreamscape where each new art piece was more surprising t

photos.caption: playa.
photos.captionKii: I remember seeing this fascinating art installation, a dark space made luminous by hanging strips of light-colored fabric. The subtle glow, perhaps from hidden lights, gave them a beautiful purple-blue hue, creating such an ethereal and tranquil atmosphere. It felt like stepping into a soft, otherworldly curtain of light, a truly captivating experience among the many creative displays in the desert city at night. You can even see a hint of another dark net-like structure at the bottom right, adding to the mysterious feel.

photos.caption: ness..
photos.captionKii: I remember the profound sense of immersion inside this particular art installation, where the darkness was broken only by these gently glowing, hanging fabric strips. The soft green light washing over them created such an ethereal and otherworldly atmosphere, truly feeling like stepping into a different realm within the desert. It was so calming and captivating, a perfect example of how these temporary structures could transform the environment and evoke such unique feelings, especially at night.

photos.caption: awesome...
photos.captionKii: I remember stepping into this incredibly immersive art installation, likely at night, where long strips of light-colored fabric hung from above, creating such an ethereal and captivating space. The way the light played off the fabric, making some sections glow brightly while others faded into shadow, was just mesmerizing. It felt like walking through a cloud or a curtain of light, adding to the otherworldly atmosphere of the desert city after dark. I loved how these temporary structures could completely transform the environment and evoke such unique feelings.

I remember taking a step back to really appreciate the incredible scale and energy of the desert city at night. The sky was so dark and vast above us, but below, the playa came alive with a breathtaking array of lights. Those bright green laser beams cutting across the landscape were particularly striking, adding such a dynamic element to the scene. You could see all the different art installations and camps twinkling in the distance, each one a little beacon of creativity and life. It was just such a magical and expansive feeling, standing there and taking in the entire illuminated panorama.

I remember looking up at these incredible, towering plumes of fire or intense light cutting through the night sky. The heat was palpable, and you could see all the illuminated dust and particles dancing in the air, making the whole scene feel so alive and dynamic. It was another amazing example of the powerful, ephemeral art installations that truly came to life after dark in the desert. I wanted to capture the raw energy and spectacle of it all, how these massive flames lit up the darkness and added to the electrifying atmosphere of the night.

photos.caption: Wah, look who we found!
photos.captionKii: I remember capturing this fantastic moment with a couple of friends, or maybe people I just met, amidst the vibrant energy of the festival at night. Everyone was dressed in such amazing, creative costumes, really embracing the spirit of the temporary desert city. The man on the left stood out with his bright purple hat, white jumpsuit, and cool goggles, while the two women beside him looked equally festive with their unique outfits, goggles, and accessories. You can even see the dust covering one of their faces, a true sign of the playa! The background shows the bustling crowd and even a glimp

photos.caption: For Kari
photos.captionKii: I remember seeing this deeply personal message written on what appeared to be a wooden art installation, adding another layer to the communal experience. The main circular text, signed by 'MEL,' spoke of a friendship that changed a life for the good, despite unspoken words and shared blame. It was a really poignant and raw expression of emotion. Below it, the scrawled 'HAHA HAHA HEH' felt like a contrasting, perhaps ironic, touch, capturing the complex mix of feelings and expressions found throughout the temporary city. I wanted to capture it because it showed how people used the art as a canv

I remember walking into this incredible, ornate wooden structure and finding this deeply moving scene at its center. There was a massive altar or shrine, absolutely piled high with countless personal offerings—photos, notes, clothing items, trinkets, and small mementos left by others. It felt like a communal space for remembrance or reflection, with so many people quietly gathered around, sitting and observing. The intricate details of the wooden architecture surrounding us added to the sacred feeling of the space, making it a powerful and poignant moment amidst all the other experiences.

I remember looking up at this absolutely massive and intricately detailed art installation, feeling awestruck by its craftsmanship. It appeared to be made of a light, natural material, perhaps wood or compressed sand, carved or cut into incredible patterns resembling ornate filigree and swirling designs. It had a grand, architectural feel, almost like a temple or an elaborate gateway, standing tall against the partly cloudy sky. The sheer scale and the dedication to such fine detail in the middle of the desert were truly remarkable, and I wanted to capture its unique grandeur.

photos.caption: This is made of zip ties
photos.captionKii: I remember stepping inside this incredible, intricate white art installation at night. It was like being enveloped by a massive, organic structure made of countless spiky or thorny filaments, illuminated from below, creating such a unique and intimate space. There were quite a few of us inside, lounging and chatting, truly becoming part of the artwork. The ambient glow against the dark desert sky made it feel like a completely different world, a perfect spot to pause and connect amidst all the other amazing experiences.

I remember looking down and capturing this wide shot of the playa floor, admiring the rich, reddish-brown hues under what seemed to be ambient light, perhaps from the sunset or a nearby installation. It wasn't just flat dirt; the surface had a subtle, organic texture with ripples and slight undulations, almost like a vast, natural canvas. The warm glow from the top left made the ground itself feel alive, radiating a unique, almost otherworldly energy. It was a moment to appreciate the raw, earthy beauty of this temporary city's foundation, even in its simplest details.

photos.caption: Sunset over the Playa — Burning Man 2012
photos.captionKii: I remember capturing this wide shot of the vast temporary desert city either at dawn or dusk, as the sky was painted with beautiful warm hues, gradually fading into lighter blue. The entire camp spread out across the dusty playa, a dense collection of tents, RVs, and various structures under this dramatic sky. What truly made this moment unique was that peculiar, dark, isolated cloud suspended above the horizon, almost like a plume of smoke, adding a surreal and captivating element to the already extraordinary landscape. I could see people still moving around, some on bicycles, amidst the camp

photos.caption: Art hangout
photos.captionKii: I remember taking a moment to relax under one of the unique art installations. I had my feet propped up, enjoying the incredible view of the sprawling temporary city in the distance, all under a perfectly clear, bright blue sky. The intricate white, cut-out patterns of the structure I was under created beautiful shadows, and I could see other people nearby, also taking in the scene. It was a perfect break to simply observe the vastness and creativity of this place from a comfortable spot.

I remember taking this wide shot to capture another perspective of the vast playa, with its incredible art pieces spread out under the brilliant blue sky. In the foreground, there was this simple, almost stark white pole or candle-like object, a subtle contrast against the huge scale of everything else. My eyes were drawn to that striking, multi-faceted blue geometric sculpture, reflecting the light, and further back, the unique light blue and white building with its distinct tower. I also spotted that fascinating, intricately woven wooden sphere, adding another layer of artistic wonder. You c

photos.caption: "Is this wig on right?" "Yeah!" (It wasn't. :))
photos.captionKii: I remember capturing this vibrant moment within the sprawling tent city. A woman, really embracing the playful spirit of the event, was wearing a bright pink wig and cool sunglasses, standing confidently amidst a colorful array of temporary homes. Behind her, you can see a striking conical tent with pink and white patterns, and beyond that, more diverse structures and tents stretching out under the clear blue sky, including a prominent large, conical building in the distance. It was fascinating to see all the individual expressions and how everyone set up their camps.

photos.caption: Toward 6 o'clock
photos.captionKii: I remember taking this wide shot to really capture the sprawling scale and unique atmosphere of this incredible temporary desert city. From this vantage point, I could see countless tents, RVs, and various temporary structures spreading out across the vast, light-colored playa under a perfectly clear blue sky. The distant mountains blurred into the horizon, emphasizing the remote setting. It was truly an overwhelming and awe-inspiring sight, witnessing how a whole vibrant community, dotted with numerous camps and art pieces, had sprung up in the middle of nowhere.

photos.caption: Inside Bank of America
photos.captionKii: I remember looking up into the bright blue sky through the striking red framework of what appeared to be a modern architectural piece or an abstract art installation. The geometric lines and angular panels of the red structure created a fascinating perspective, almost like a giant, open-ended funnel or a skylight. The sunlight hit it perfectly, casting strong shadows and highlighting its bold, contemporary design. It was such a visually captivating structure.

photos.caption: Memorials at the temple
photos.captionKii: I remember coming across this incredible, intricately carved wooden structure, possibly an art installation or a memorial, out in what felt like a vast, open desert. The details were just mesmerizing, with so many patterns and shapes defining its form. On its surface, there were various small wooden sculptures, some resembling stars, along with several heartfelt messages and cards, one even proudly displaying the word "LOVE." There was also a large poster board filled with images and text, possibly depicting dogs or wolves, which made me think it might have been a tribute. The clear blue sky o

photos.caption: Paisley helps with the shade structure
photos.captionKii: I remember seeing my partner completely engrossed in a crafting project, kneeling on the warm wooden floor. She had such a focused yet content smile on her face as she carefully measured and marked a piece of dark fabric with a ruler and pen. Her cozy brown cardigan and a light blue scarf gave her a creative, relaxed look. Right next to her, our dog, with its beautiful reddish-brown and white merle coat, was perfectly content, snoozing peacefully on a piece of cloth. It was such a sweet, quiet moment, full of domestic warmth and creativity.

photos.caption: "Holy hippie shit!" -@meghantosh as we finish making dinner :)
photos.captionKii: I remember putting together this vibrant spread of healthy food, all laid out on our wooden table. There was a big bowl of fresh greens for a salad, and next to it, another bowl overflowing with what looked like a fresh fruit salsa, probably with strawberries or tomatoes. I had also cooked up a batch of quinoa, which was in a white bowl, ready to be served. My favorite roasted vegetables were also on the plate: tender asparagus spears and some perfectly caramelized Brussels sprouts. And to complete the meal, a small bowl of green, olive-like bites. I even had a half avocado ready to go. It fel

photos.caption: Birthday petting zoo in the parklet. In case you had any doubt that this was the best coffee shop ever. <3 — Four Barrel 4th Birthday party
photos.captionKii: I remember seeing these three little ones completely captivated by this large tortoise! They were all gathered around it, reaching out with such gentle curiosity to touch its rough, patterned shell. We were in what looked like an indoor animal pen, with wood shavings on the ground, and I could even spot a goat's leg in the background, hinting at other animals nearby. It was such a sweet moment, watching their genuine interest and tender interaction with the tortoise.

photos.caption:
"Nice guys don't have reddit accounts" — Seen at SF Center for the Book. Those are for Papergirl SF! 

papergirl-sf.com/
photos.captionKii: I remember seeing these fresh prints laid out, likely just pulled from the press. The bold, blocky letters spelled out a rather humorous and memorable phrase: 'NICE GUYS DON'T HAVE REDDIT ACCOUNTS.' It was such a striking piece of typography, and the slight impression on the paper made it clear it was a physical print, probably from a letterpress. In the background, I could see hints of a broader project, something about community distribution 'PAPERBOY STYLE!' on November 11th, which added an interesting context to the workshop or studio I was in. It felt really cool to see the actual output

photos.caption: Dead leads and lost type
photos.captionKii: I remember seeing this fascinating corner of what seemed to be a letterpress print shop, still completely immersed in the world of vintage typography. My eyes were drawn to the bright red plastic bin, labeled distinctly, "DEAD LEADS & SLUGS ONLY" with a humorous and stern warning: "*ONLY TYPE IN HELL BUCKET!*" It was full of various metal pieces, clearly the remnants from printing. Next to it, I noticed another container, partially visible, with a green label simply stating "LOST TYPE." The wooden shelves in the background, stacked high with what looked like more type blocks, added to the auth

photos.caption: Drawer full of Futura <3 <3
photos.captionKii: I remember being absolutely fascinated by this wooden drawer, brimming with various metal letterpress type blocks. Each compartment held different characters—I could make out capital 'A's, 'M's, and 'W's, alongside numbers and symbols like dollar signs and ampersands, all standing ready. It was incredible to see these individual pieces of metal, knowing the meticulous work that went into hand-setting them to create text in the days before digital printing. It felt like stepping back in time, and I just had to capture this intricate collection of vintage typography.

photos.caption: Getting back in it!
photos.captionKii: I remember looking down at my trusty black Adidas soccer boots, all laced up and ready for action on the vibrant green artificial turf. The classic white stripes stood out against the dark leather, and I could feel the anticipation of a good game or practice. After watching the Olympics yesterday, it felt great to be out on the field myself, even if it was just for a casual kick-around. It was a clear sign I was about to get active and enjoy some time outdoors.

photos.caption: The USA v JPN women's gold medal match is one of the best soccer matches I've watched. Intense!!
photos.captionKii: I vividly remember sitting down to watch this intense moment of the Women's Olympic Soccer Final between USA and Japan! The score was 2-0 in favor of the USA during the second half, around the 57-minute mark. It was such a thrilling game, and I captured this shot of the action on my TV, where a US player in a dark jersey with number 16 was battling for the ball against a Japanese player in red. You can even see the NBC Olympics logo in the corner, marking the significance of the event. The energy was palpable, even through the screen, making it an unforgettable moment of the 2012 London Olympi

photos.caption: Summer Corn (Red barn remix)
photos.captionKii: I remember seeing this beautiful, classic rural scene on a perfectly clear day. Two vibrant red barns, each with a bright green roof, stood out against the deep blue sky, partially framed by a field of tall, green cornstalks in the foreground. The cornfield stretched out, making the barns feel nestled deep in the countryside. There was a patch of bright green grass to the left of the corn, and a single leafy tree visible in the distance on the horizon. It felt so peaceful and picturesque, a lovely contrast to the previous day's activities.

photos.caption: "If you put Bud-Light in the koozie it'll look like a Pepsi. That's what I used to do." -- good advice from @natalie_eyink's dad ;)
photos.captionKii: I remember holding onto this vibrant red koozie, proudly displaying 'Coldwater COMMUNITY PICNIC Coldwater, Ohio'! It was a warm and sunny day, and having my drink in this koozie kept it perfectly cool. My hand, with its bright pink nail polish, stood out against the red fabric. In the blurry background, I could make out other people enjoying themselves and the asphalt ground, indicating we were right in the middle of the picnic activities. It was a perfect little detail that really captured the spirit of the event.

I remember the exhilarating energy of this carnival ride as dusk settled over the festival! The ride, with its vibrant pink and yellow lights, was spinning rapidly, creating a beautiful blur against the twilight sky. The sky itself was a lovely gradient of purples, pinks, and oranges, making the scene feel magical. You can even see some stadium lights and trees in the background, hinting at the larger fairgrounds. It was such a lively and fun sight, perfectly capturing the festive atmosphere as the evening continued.

I remember enjoying this delicious-looking soft-serve ice cream cone! The vanilla swirl was perfectly high and creamy, and the warm, golden light of the late afternoon or early evening made it look even more inviting. My hand, with its bright red nail polish, was holding the classic waffle cone. In the background, you can see what looks like a white-paneled ice cream or concession stand, with various bottles on shelves, bathed in that same beautiful golden hour glow. It was a perfect sweet treat.

photos.caption: 11:30 on a Sunday. Do you know how your Beer Tree is?? #ccp12
photos.captionKii: I remember seeing this hilarious 'Beer Tree' at the festival as night fell! It was literally a tree trunk surrounded by an orange construction fence, and inside, people had piled up all their empty beer cans and bottles. There was a hand-written sign that just said 'BEER TREE', which perfectly summed it up. The ground was covered in grass, and in the background, you could see the festive atmosphere with tents lit up and crowds of people enjoying themselves under the artificial lights. It was such a unique and funny sight amidst all the other activities, really adding to the quirky charm of the

photos.caption: Cover band. WADDUP.
photos.captionKii: I remember the energy of this evening at the festival, watching a band perform live on stage! They were playing under a festive red and white striped tent, with the stage illuminated by bright red and white lights, really setting the mood. There was a big crowd in front of the stage, all gathered to enjoy the music, creating such a lively atmosphere. It was a perfect way to experience the festival at night, enjoying some great tunes and the company of others.

photos.caption: Chuck-a-Luck
photos.captionKii: I remember the fun atmosphere at the festival when night fell, with people trying their luck at various carnival games under the tents. It was cool to see this big wooden wheel game, with one man in a yellow cap standing casually by it, and another man in a plaid shirt intently focused on a long table game that looked like shuffleboard or a coin toss, covered with little white chips. The scene felt so lively with the artificial lights creating a warm glow, and you can even spot someone on the right holding a drink, really capturing the relaxed, celebratory mood of the evening's festivities.

photos.caption: Corn BINGO!
photos.captionKii: I remember this fun moment at the festival, gathering around for a game of bingo! It was such a classic, community-focused activity. I loved that we were using these traditional corn kernels as markers on the bingo cards, which felt so authentic and charming. The yellow kernels were scattered all over the wooden surface, some neatly placed on the numbers like '3,' '26,' and '50,' while others were waiting for their turn. It really added to the old-fashioned, cheerful atmosphere of the day, a perfect way to relax and enjoy the lively event.

photos.caption: Giant stein
photos.captionKii: I remember catching this fun parade float at the festival. It was designed to look like a giant beer stein, covered with what looked like pretzels or bread rolls, which made it so unique! There was someone on top, waving to the crowd, adding to the festive energy. The float even had a little white picket fence around its base and a sign on the back that said 'Oct 5-7,' possibly promoting another event or marking a special date. As it drove by, I could see people relaxing and watching from the grassy roadside, sitting in chairs under the shade of the trees. It really captured the lively spirit

photos.caption: Kendall Woo winning cake!
photos.captionKii: I remember this sweet moment at the festival, seeing this adorable little girl beaming as she proudly held a pan of what looked like delicious brownies or a chocolate sheet cake. She had such a genuine, happy smile, and there was even a little smudge on her cheek, which just added to her charm. It felt like she was so excited to be holding her prize or perhaps a treat to share. The background with the tree trunk and glimpses of other people suggested we were still enjoying the lively outdoor event, making this a perfect capture of the day's joyous and community-focused spirit.

photos.caption: Gambling for cakes at the Cake Stand!
photos.captionKii: I remember finding this charming 'Cake Stand' at the festival, which was such a classic fair attraction. The sign clearly stated '25¢ per paddle,' and it was all about trying your luck on that big numbered wheel to win one of those delicious-looking baked goods. There were so many cakes and pies, all neatly packaged on shelves, just waiting to be won. I could see other people, mostly women, gathered around, some checking out the treats and others probably waiting for their turn to spin. The red and white striped tent overhead really added to the festive atmosphere, and you could tell it was a

photos.caption: Beer Tree. 6:45 pm. God bless America.
photos.captionKii: I remember stumbling upon this quirky 'Beer Tree' during what looked like a lively outdoor festival or gathering. The sign, simply saying 'BEER TREE,' was quite a sight, especially with all the empty cans collected inside the orange mesh fence around the tree trunk. It was clearly a fun, casual event, with people relaxing under various tents in the background. I could see the red and white striped tents, along with a darker green one, and lots of people milling around. It captured the lighthearted, communal spirit of the day, a funny little detail amidst all the activity.

photos.caption: Spirograph!
photos.captionKii: I remember coming across this fantastic vintage Spirograph box, probably during an outing to an antique shop or perhaps while tidying up. The bright red packaging with its classic design immediately caught my eye, promising 'a million marvelous patterns' that one could 'DRAW.' It brought back a flood of memories seeing the illustrations of kids happily creating those intricate geometric designs, along with clear examples of the colorful patterns. The box even listed the contents, like '18 Transparent Plastic Wheels' and '4 Ballpoint Pens.' It was a real blast from the past, reminding me of the

photos.caption: Grandma's house :)
photos.captionKii: I remember seeing our house bathed in this incredibly warm, golden light during either sunrise or sunset. The way the light hit the white siding made everything glow, and I loved the intricate shadows the tree branches cast across the front. It looked so peaceful and inviting, with the dark shutters contrasting nicely with the bright walls. I could see the house number, 507, clearly marked above the door, and the lawn looked perfectly green. It was a beautiful, quiet moment, just enjoying the familiar sight of home.

photos.caption: Delta times
photos.captionKii: I remember stopping by the Union Point Marina Bar & Grill on a bright, clear day. The big red sign with its bold lettering really stood out against the brilliant blue sky. It looked like a perfect evening to sit outside, with those vibrant red umbrellas shading the blue picnic tables arranged on the wooden deck. I could see other people already enjoying their meals and drinks in the relaxed marina atmosphere. It was such a nice, casual spot right by the water, perfect for unwinding and enjoying the good weather.

photos.caption: Typesetting and leading @ The Washington Post D.C. #lategram
photos.captionKii: I remember coming across this really cool display of old-fashioned printing blocks, probably in a museum, while exploring the city's historical aspects. It was fascinating to see the individual metal letters, called 'type,' all neatly arranged on a ruler or composing stick to form a word. I could clearly make out 'Washington' spelled out in that intricate, almost gothic font. It gave me a real appreciation for how much effort went into printing back in the day, especially seeing all the other loose type blocks scattered in the background. It made me think about the history of communication and

photos.caption: Brutal (airport remix white label)
photos.captionKii: I remember looking up and admiring the incredible architecture of the museum we were in. The ceiling had this smooth, curving shape, almost like a massive wave or wing, made of a concrete-like material. I really liked how the huge windows were angled, letting in so much natural light and offering a glimpse of the blue sky and clouds outside. The way the light played off the structural beams created these cool shadows and lines. It felt really open and modern, a perfect setting for all the amazing things we were seeing that day.

I remember taking this close-up shot of the space shuttle's side, highlighting the iconic 'United States' insignia and the NASA logo. It was incredible to see the name 'Discovery' clearly printed on its wing, just below the American flag. From this angle, you can really appreciate the sleek design and the precise markings that adorned these magnificent vehicles. The faint lighting in the museum hall, along with other suspended exhibits in the background, added to the awe-inspiring atmosphere of being so near to a piece of history that had traveled to space and back.

photos.caption: Under her wing
photos.captionKii: I remember taking this really wide shot of the space shuttle, trying to capture its immense scale within the museum hall. You can really see how huge it is, spanning across the frame, with its underside illuminated from below. I particularly noticed the intricate structural details of its belly and wings from this angle. In the background, there's a crowd of people, which further emphasizes just how massive this piece of engineering is. It was also cool to spot the astronaut figure suspended from the ceiling in the upper right, almost as if he's floating alongside the shuttle. This view truly

photos.caption: Tiles
photos.captionKii: I remember getting a really close look at the thermal protection tiles on the space shuttle. You can see the intricate pattern of the gray squares, some of them looking a bit weathered from their journeys. It's fascinating to see how they fit together, and I noticed a few darker, black tiles arranged in a pattern where some seem to be missing, perhaps indicating a different type of material or a section where tiles were removed for display. Each tile felt like a piece of engineering marvel, designed to withstand the extreme temperatures of reentry. It really brought home the complexity of spac

photos.caption: Hello, there.
photos.captionKii: I remember standing in awe before this immense space shuttle, likely during my D.C. trip, marveling at its sheer scale and engineering. From this angle, you can really appreciate the massive engines and the intricate design of the tail fin. The 'United States' insignia proudly displayed on its side, along with the NASA logo, brought home the incredible achievements of space exploration. It was incredible to be so close to such a significant piece of history, displayed in what I recall was a huge hangar or museum hall, with other space artifacts suspended above.

photos.caption: (@benward)
photos.captionKii: I remember seeing this really interesting interactive display that detailed the history and impact of Twitter, likely at a museum in D.C. It explained how Twitter launched in 2006 and quickly became a significant platform for spreading news, even highlighting specific milestones like 'The First Tweet' and 'Twitter Goes Mainstream.' It was fascinating to see how it evolved from being 'derided as a waste of time' to becoming a major player in new media, revolutionizing how information travels. The display even showed a mock-up of the Twitter login page, encouraging users to 'Follow your interest

photos.caption: Oh hey, guys.
photos.captionKii: I remember coming across a really cool interactive display about Apple's history, likely in a museum during my D.C. trip. It chronicled the company's innovations, showing the evolution from the iPod to the iPhone and iPad, and highlighting Steve Jobs. There was also a detailed section on iTunes, explaining how it revolutionized digital music with its online store. It was fascinating to see the timeline of how technology has shaped our lives, laid out so clearly on the screen, detailing how digital media was put into consumer's pockets.

photos.caption: Newspaper corrections on the wall of the bathroom. Great idea. :)
photos.captionKii: I remember seeing this fascinating historical display embedded in a tiled wall, likely in a museum or public building in Washington D.C. It was a framed replica of a newspaper headline from February 24, 1976, boldly stating "Ford, Reagan Neck in Presidential Primary." It was cool to stumble upon such a specific piece of political history, offering a brief glimpse into a past era. I thought it was interesting enough to capture.

photos.caption: @leyink: Outside on the balcony on a summer night with some wine looking back at the Capitol.
photos.captionKii: I remember gazing at the majestic U.S. Capitol Building, beautifully lit up against the dark night sky. It was such an iconic and breathtaking sight, seeing the dome glow from a distance. The surrounding city lights twinkled faintly below, adding to the serene and grand atmosphere of the evening. It really captured the essence of being in Washington D.C. at night.

photos.caption: The original "Migrant Mother" by Dorothea Lange. No big deal. (!) — The Library of Congress pulled this out during a party.
photos.captionKii: I remember seeing a display of the iconic 'Migrant Mother' photograph by Dorothea Lange during my exploration in Washington D.C. It was framed and presented with information, likely in a library or museum, as the text below it partly mentioned it as the 'Most Famous Photo in the Library.' The powerful image of the woman with her children truly resonated with me, and it was incredible to see such a significant piece of photographic history up close. I also noticed another display next to it, possibly a book cover or poster, adding to the cultural richness of the place.

photos.caption: Wikimedia party
photos.captionKii: I remember looking up in awe at the incredibly ornate and majestic ceiling of a grand building in Washington D.C. The architecture was breathtaking, with layer upon layer of detailed craftsmanship. I could see beautifully painted murals, intricate geometric patterns, and elegant arches supported by classical columns. The light softly illuminated the rich colors and textures, making the entire space feel truly magnificent and historical. It was such a stunning sight that I had to capture this perspective, emphasizing the scale and artistry above me.

photos.caption: Lines and lines
photos.captionKii: I remember looking up at the sky as I was either waiting at the station or beginning my journey. The electrical wires crisscrossing overhead against the soft, cloudy sky really caught my eye. The sky had beautiful subtle colors, hinting at dusk or dawn, creating a serene backdrop to the concrete structures of the station or nearby buildings. It perfectly captured the feeling of being in transit, with the anticipation of the journey ahead, or the reflection of the journey just taken.

photos.caption: Handwritten train ticket
photos.captionKii: I remember holding my Amtrak ticket in hand, ready for a journey from BWI, Maryland, to Washington, D.C. The ticket, dated July 9, 2012, clearly outlines the details of my trip: Train 193, departing at 8:25 PM, with a total fare of $20.00. I made sure to capture this detail, as it marked the beginning of my travel to the nation's capital.

I remember stopping at a place that clearly advertised 'WINDOW SERVICE' for 'ICE CREAM' and 'COLD DRINKS'. The signs were painted directly onto the glass, with cheerful white letters and bold red wavy lines. There was even a prominent red arrow pointing right, presumably directing customers to the service window! I captured this sign because it perfectly summed up the simple pleasure of grabbing a cool treat on a warm day. Through the window, I can faintly see a reflection, and below the sign, there were neat stacks of straws ready for customers, adding to the charm.

photos.caption: Since 1964
photos.captionKii: I remember catching this classic sign for 'Airline Dairy Creme' on a bright, clear day. The vibrant blue and white lettering really popped against the light blue sky, with just a few soft clouds adding texture. It always brings back memories of stopping for a cool treat, perhaps an ice cream or a shake, on a warm summer day.

photos.caption: Formation
photos.captionKii: I remember looking up again today and catching this incredible sight! Three fighter jets were soaring across the sky in a tight formation, their dark silhouettes stark against the bright blue. The clouds were sparse and fluffy, making for a dramatic backdrop as they flew. It was such an impressive display of aerial prowess, and I'm glad I captured this moment of them flying together.

photos.caption: Blue Angels. 6.
photos.captionKii: I remember looking up at the sky on this day and catching this amazing sight! There were several planes flying in a perfect formation, leaving behind these crisp, parallel contrails across the blue and cloudy sky. It looked like quite a synchronized aerial display. I also noticed a fainter, more spiraling trail off to the left, which added another interesting element to the vast expanse above.

I remember this amazing spread on the grill during the lakeside gathering! There was such a fantastic variety of food cooking. I could see juicy chicken and vegetable skewers, several plump sausages, and some large, savory portobello mushrooms. And look, even some oysters on the half shell were cooking right there on the grill, adding to the incredible aroma. It was truly a feast, perfectly complementing the relaxed outdoor vibe of the day.

photos.caption: So tasty
photos.captionKii: I remember this delicious plate of fresh oysters I had at the outdoor gathering. They were served beautifully on the half shell, topped with a flavorful green herb sauce, and accompanied by fresh lime wedges, all arranged on a vibrant red and white paper plate. It was such a treat to enjoy these by the water, perfectly complementing the grilled food and the relaxed atmosphere of the day.

photos.caption: Chris manning the epic ribs
photos.captionKii: I remember this moment at the outdoor gathering, watching someone expertly tending to the grill. There was plenty of smoke rising, carrying the delicious aroma of whatever he was cooking—it looked like some ribs or similar meat, being flipped with tongs. He was wearing a red cap and a dark long-sleeved shirt, really focused on the task. Next to him, a woman in sunglasses and a red top was standing, and in the background, more people were mingling, including a young boy with blond hair. The sandy ground added to the relaxed, casual vibe of the whole day.

photos.caption: Oyster time!
photos.captionKii: I remember this fantastic outdoor gathering by the water. It was such a beautiful day with clear skies and fluffy clouds. There were so many people enjoying themselves, mingling and grabbing food from the tables set up in front of that quaint white building. I noticed the cool fish decoration mounted on the side of the building, which added a nice touch to the lakeside (or bayside) vibe. In the distance, you could see rolling hills, and a large, stately tree provided some welcome shade over part of the crowd. It felt like a perfect summer day for socializing and relaxing outdoors.

photos.caption: Cool to see an American bar packed for #gergre #euro2012
photos.captionKii: I remember being at this lively bar, soaking in the atmosphere. The place was bustling with people, some sitting right at the long counter where all the bottles and beer taps were lined up, and others further back at tables. I could see a couple of TVs on the walls, one with what looked like a sports logo, and a cool mural depicting a crowd of people. There was also a quirky sign above the bar that seemed to say "NAPER TANDY". It felt like a great spot to grab a drink and socialize, with everyone enjoying themselves.

I remember looking around while waiting or dining and spotting this incredibly unique decoration: a whole car mounted high up on the light yellow wall! It was such an unexpected and quirky touch to the place. The car was this cool light grey sedan, and even its wheels had this distinctive dotted pattern. Below it, there was also a wooden sign shaped like an arrow pointing to the 'PATIO,' probably indicating outdoor seating. It really made the restaurant's interior feel special and memorable, definitely worth capturing.

I remember seeing this charming 'Please WAIT TO BE SEATED' sign, knowing we were about to enjoy a meal. The wooden board with its bright red frame and the whimsical illustrations of people relaxing—one by a campfire and another in a rocking chair—definitely caught my eye. It gave the place a cozy, welcoming vibe even before we sat down. I snapped a picture of it while we were waiting to get a table, anticipating some good food.

photos.caption: OMG the world makes DONUT MUFFINS!
photos.captionKii: I remember stumbling upon this irresistible display of baked goods! In the background, there were these intriguing 'Donut Muffins' piled high on a white tray, looking deliciously coated in sugar. And right in front, another tray was brimming with what looked like fruit muffins, with the vibrant red of berries or possibly rhubarb peeking out from the tops. It was hard to choose just one; they all looked so tempting behind the glass, making for a perfect treat or breakfast stop.

photos.caption: Hello sunset
photos.captionKii: I remember seeing this breathtaking sunset and just having to capture it. The sky was ablaze with these incredible streaks of fiery pink and red, cutting across a deep, rich blue. It looked almost painted, with some darker clouds adding texture to the vibrant colors. It was a really beautiful moment, just soaking in the natural beauty as the day ended.

photos.caption: Fresh shuffle board
photos.captionKii: I remember spotting the distinctive El Rio logo on this wooden surface during our hangout. The black oval with the stylized gold 'El Rio' lettering really stood out as a cool detail, reflecting the relaxed vibe of the place. It was a nice close-up to capture, adding to the memories of that evening.

I remember this lively evening at El Rio, enjoying the outdoor patio scene. There was such a relaxed vibe, with people scattered around, chatting and holding drinks. I especially remember seeing a woman standing near the back, holding up a colorful patterned scarf, almost like she was dancing or just expressing herself. In the foreground, a few guys were chilling on the wooden deck, drinks in hand, engrossed in conversation. The lush plants along the wall and the distinctive 'El Rio' sign really set the scene for a warm, social night out.

photos.caption: DE v. NL with Berlin <3
photos.captionKii: I remember this night, completely engrossed in the soccer match playing on the massive screens at the bar. The entire wall was practically a giant television, split into several panels showing different angles of the game, which was really cool. Below the screens, I could see the glow of the well-stocked bar, and there were quite a few people sitting on stools, their backs to me, all focused on the action. The red ceiling lights added to the dimmed, concentrated atmosphere of everyone watching the game. It was a classic sports bar experience, enjoying the excitement of live sports with a crowd

photos.caption: Fanboys out in force! #wwdc
photos.captionKii: I remember this bustling scene outside Moscone West, completely packed with people eagerly waiting to get in. The huge Apple logo was clearly visible through the glass facade, signaling that it was WWDC, Apple's Worldwide Developers Conference. The excitement was palpable, with such a long line stretching down the street on what looked like a bright, sunny day. It was quite an impressive sight, truly capturing the energy of such a major tech event.

photos.caption: Boardwalkin'
photos.captionKii: I remember looking up at these bright turquoise gondolas against the perfectly clear, brilliant blue sky, watching people enjoy the ride. The two cables stretching across the frame, with the gondolas suspended, gave such a sense of height and freedom. It was a lovely sunny day, and seeing the dangling legs of the passengers made me think of the fun and breezy feeling of soaring above everything, taking in the views. It was such a picturesque scene, truly capturing the essence of a fun day out.

photos.caption: You never get old
photos.captionKii: I remember this vibrant bar scene, completely immersed in the cool glow of neon lights. The blue and red hues reflecting off the countless bottles behind the bar created a truly energetic and lively atmosphere. I could see the 'Pints on Tap' menu clearly displayed, listing options like Racer IPA, Trumer Pilsner, Red Seal Ale, and Liberty Ale, making it tough to choose just one. It was definitely a fun night out, enjoying the ambiance and perhaps some good company.

photos.caption: In transit #venustransit
photos.captionKii: I remember this incredible view through the telescope, likely of the sun, which was set up during the outdoor viewing event. The dark filter gave the sun a muted, yet powerful glow, and I could clearly see a prominent dark spot, possibly a sunspot or even the transit of Venus, and another smaller, fainter dot. It was absolutely fascinating to get such a close-up look at our star, especially after seeing all the telescopes in the park. The green grass in the background reminds me exactly where I was.

photos.caption: Venus transit telescope party #venustransit
photos.captionKii: I remember being at this lively outdoor gathering, surrounded by fellow enthusiasts and their impressive telescopes. It was a bright, sunny day in the park, perfect for some public viewing, possibly of the sun or other celestial bodies during the day. People were casually strolling around, peering into the scopes, and chatting, all enjoying the shared experience on the green grass under the clear sky. It felt like a wonderful community event, bringing people together through a shared curiosity about the cosmos.

photos.caption: Rye crumbs, cheese curd, and peas. Adventures in dinner with @timoni #lategram
photos.captionKii: I remember this exquisitely presented dish, a true example of modern culinary artistry. It was served in a pristine white bowl, featuring a delicate frothy, light brown base that enveloped various intriguing components. On one side, there was a textural mound of earthy, brown crumble, while soft, white elements, possibly a cheese or curd, were dotted in the center. A fresh, vibrant green shoot stood gracefully on the right, adding a pop of color and freshness, making the whole plate look like a miniature edible landscape. It was a memorable and sophisticated gastronomic experience.

photos.caption: Adventures in Monday night dinner with @timoni #lategram
photos.captionKii: I remember being served these absolutely delightful little bites, which were presented so beautifully on spoons. Each held a finely chopped mix of ingredients, perhaps a tartare or a rich pâté, with a dollop of creamy white sauce and a streak of vibrant green beneath. They were delicately garnished with tiny white flowers and fresh herbs, making them look like miniature works of art. The rustic, brownish plate they rested on added to the sophisticated feel of this gourmet moment. It was truly a treat for both the eyes and the palate.

photos.caption: Fisherman's dozen
photos.captionKii: I remember enjoying this absolutely delightful plate of fresh oysters, perfectly complemented by lemon wedges and that vibrant green sauce, possibly a mignonette, right by the water. It was such a relaxing scene, with a bottle of white wine and a beer chilling nearby, alongside some Tabasco for an extra kick. I was also engrossed in a book, 'The Bohemian Grove and Other High-Jinks,' which made for a perfect afternoon read. The gentle sparkle of the water in the background and the glimpse of a boat really added to the serene atmosphere. It was such a pleasant moment, soaking up the sun and savo

photos.caption: Natural habitat
photos.captionKii: I remember stepping into this wonderfully cluttered and cozy bookstore, immediately drawn to its quiet atmosphere. I caught a moment of a person completely absorbed in reading, perched comfortably on a stool with their bare feet resting. They were surrounded by stacks of books on towering shelves, which felt like a maze of stories. Above, I noticed the 'AMERICANA CALIFORNIA' sign and a fascinating framed artwork of vintage airplanes, adding to the unique character of this literary hideaway. It was the perfect escape for an afternoon.

photos.caption: The @kidhack Winchstagram
photos.captionKii: I remember watching this person effortlessly glide across the water on what looks like a wakeboard or a skimboard, creating such a fantastic spray behind them. The sun was out, making the water sparkle and the surrounding trees look so vibrant and green. Another person was standing in the shallows, possibly getting ready to take a turn or just enjoying the cool water. It felt like a perfect summer day for some outdoor fun on the river, enjoying the active atmosphere and the beautiful natural scenery.

photos.caption: The best of the future
photos.captionKii: I remember catching this impressive view of the iconic TWA Flight Center at JFK. Its sweeping, wing-like architecture is just incredible, truly a masterpiece of mid-century modern design that still feels so futuristic. You can really feel the sense of movement and flight captured in its structure. In the background, I could see several airplanes waiting on the tarmac under a bright, hazy sky, reminding me of the journey ahead or the one I'd just completed. It's such a memorable building and always impressive to see.

photos.caption: Empire State
photos.captionKii: I remember taking this shot of the city at night, absolutely mesmerized by the sparkling lights. The prominent bridge, beautifully illuminated, created such a striking silhouette against the dark sky, reflecting faintly on the water below. I could also make out a distant, iconic skyscraper, its spire lit up, adding to the magical feel of the urban landscape stretching out before me. It was a really beautiful and peaceful moment, capturing the vibrant energy of the city even in the stillness of the night.

I remember stepping out into this fascinating rooftop space, which felt like a natural extension of the 'Space Program: Mars' theme. There was a charming, almost makeshift wooden boardwalk winding its way through patches of green, giving it the feel of a rugged, repurposed garden. I noticed the distinctive blue barrels and other equipment scattered around, fitting perfectly with the industrial, DIY aesthetic I'd seen inside. Even under the overcast sky, the unique blend of nature and salvaged materials created a really immersive environment, adding another layer to the whole experience of what

Walking further into the exhibition, I was really struck by this incredible geodesic dome structure. It was constructed with a network of metal bars, giving it a raw, almost industrial feel that perfectly matched the 'space program' aesthetic. Parts of it were covered with what looked like translucent sheeting, adding to the sense of an ongoing, ambitious project. It was surrounded by other substantial, rusty metal frameworks, and you could see various barrels and equipment around, reinforcing the DIY, workshop-like atmosphere of the entire Mars mission concept. It was fascinating to see how t

I remember seeing this impressive sign for the "Tom Sachs SPACE PROGRAM MARS" exhibition. It was prominently displayed, with the artist's name and the title of the program standing out in that unique script, and "MARS" highlighted in red. The smaller text below mentioned "PARK AVENUE ARMORY" and "CREATIVETIME," making it clear I was at a significant art and performance space. I was really looking forward to experiencing the exhibition, which promised to be a fascinating blend of art, science, and the playful exploration of space. It felt like a significant cultural event I didn't want to miss.

photos.caption: Hello, New York!
photos.captionKii: I remember being absolutely captivated by the sheer grandeur of what I recognize as Grand Central Terminal. The vast main concourse was buzzing with activity, a sea of people moving about, creating that unique energy of a major city hub. My eyes were drawn to the towering columns, the impressive arched windows letting in light from above, and of course, the iconic large American flag hanging prominently. I also noticed the distinct information booth with its four-faced clock, a true centerpiece around which all the hustle and bustle revolved. It felt like stepping into a cinematic scene, a tru

I remember stepping into the main hall of what felt like a truly grand transportation hub. My eyes were immediately drawn upward to the incredibly ornate, patterned ceiling, with those striking spherical globe lights hanging down, casting a warm glow. In the distance, framed by a massive archway, I could see a prominent clock and a large American flag draped vertically, beautifully lit. It was clear this was a place with a lot of history and importance. Below, the bustling concourse had benches and people waiting, moving, and gathering, creating a sense of anticipation. It was a perfect captur

photos.caption: To New York! First stop New Haven. :)
photos.captionKii: I remember standing at South Station, looking up at the large train information board. The date, May 25, 2012, was clearly visible at the top, along with the current time showing 11:03 AM. It was busy, with so many destinations listed for both Amtrak and MBTA trains, like Washington, D.C., Wickford, RI, and even Chicago. I was probably trying to figure out which platform my train was departing from, or just checking the schedule. It felt like the start of a new journey, with all the possibilities that travel brings.

photos.caption: Accidentally wandered into Cambridge on graduation day!
photos.captionKii: I remember capturing this grand view of what looks like an iconic building on a university campus. The architecture was really striking, with its beautiful red brick facade, tall white columns, and a large pediment adorned with a distinctive crest that I recognize as Harvard's. Topping it all off was that elegant white cupola, standing out prominently against the bright, clear sky. It felt like I was standing in front of a place steeped in history and academic prestige. You can even see a few people near the entrance, giving a sense of the scale of this magnificent structure. It was quite impr

photos.caption: Hello, Sutro. \|/
photos.captionKii: I remember looking up and capturing this quintessential city view, where the clear blue sky met a dramatic, distinct line of fog just above the rooftops. It was fascinating to see the bright sunshine above, contrasting with the way the fog bank seemed to hover over the charming houses below. The architecture of the homes on the street was really interesting, with various roof styles—I particularly noticed a prominent red-tiled roof on the right and other pitched roofs further down. It felt like a classic scene from a city known for its unique weather patterns, making for a beautiful visual sep

photos.caption: Beach sprint!
photos.captionKii: I remember this beautiful day by the coast. My black fixed-gear bike, with its striking white wheels and handlebars, was leaning against a low concrete wall, perfectly framed by the expansive sandy beach and the ocean stretching out to the horizon. In the distance, I could just make out a few people enjoying a leisurely walk along the shoreline. The sky was bright and clear, making for such a serene and peaceful scene. It was one of those moments where everything felt right, just enjoying the simplicity of a bike ride and the beauty of the sea.

photos.caption: "The Earth Flag feels nice to wave." –from the Whole Earth Catalog
photos.captionKii: I remember coming across this fascinating newspaper or magazine clipping that detailed the story behind the 'Earth Flag.' It was really interesting to read about how John McConnell and artist Norman LaLiberte, inspired by the Apollo Earth photographs, came up with the idea for a flag that represents all people, especially since all nations and organizations have flags. The image showed the flag itself – a simple but powerful design of Earth on a dark background, complete with its staff. The text also provided details about its size, the organization 'WE' Inc., and even the original price and m

photos.caption: Bikegeist
photos.captionKii: I remember stumbling upon this really cool spot, where several bikes were artfully displayed or stored, possibly hanging up. The bright pink frame of one bike immediately caught my eye, contrasting with the black frame next to it and the white handlebars with their textured grip. They were set against a rustic wooden slatted fence, which had some interesting signs on it—I could make out what looked like a 'NO SMOKING' sign and another larger, faded poster in the background. It felt like a quintessential urban cycling scene, perhaps outside a bike shop or a cafe that caters to cyclists. I loved

photos.caption: The last time I saw @theblackkeys was 10 years ago at a The Union, a rad dive bar in Athens, OH. Still just 2 dudes from Oh-Oh-Oh-Ohio!
photos.captionKii: I remember the incredible energy of this concert, looking out from what felt like a good spot in the arena. The stage was absolutely alive with vibrant purple and blue spotlights crisscrossing and illuminating the band as they performed. You could just make out the musicians and their instruments in the bright glow. Below, the crowd was buzzing, and I loved seeing all the tiny lights from people's phones held high, creating a really magical atmosphere in the darkness. It was such a memorable night, feeling the music and being part of such a lively audience.

photos.caption: Beers and truck paintin'. The super nice dudes of San Franpsycho.
photos.captionKii: I remember walking by and spotting these guys engrossed in creating a really striking piece of street art. They were painting bold white letters, which seemed to spell out part of 'AMERICAN PSYCHO' or something similar, onto a large black surface, possibly a truck or a mobile art installation, right there on the sidewalk. It was a bright, sunny day, and the urban scene around them was buzzing, with a parking meter nearby and even some kids in the background watching the action. It felt like a spontaneous and vibrant moment of creativity unfolding in the city, which I loved capturing.

photos.caption: West County
photos.captionKii: I remember seeing this rustic white wooden fence, bathed in the beautiful golden light of what must have been sunrise or sunset. The paint was peeling, giving it such character against the vast, green field that stretched out under a clear, pale blue sky. It was such a tranquil and peaceful scene, really capturing the quiet beauty of the countryside. I loved how the warm light highlighted the texture of the old wood and the endless expanse of the landscape. It felt like a moment of calm and natural beauty.

I remember this beautiful, clear day at a pebble beach. The sun was shining brightly, creating a shimmering reflection on the vast ocean stretching out to the horizon. The entire shoreline was covered in smooth, dark pebbles, and the distant coastline had a gentle haze. I found this weathered wooden board, perhaps driftwood, and someone had arranged a collection of smaller, dark pebbles and what looked like a crab shell on it. It felt like a little piece of art left by the waves, and I loved capturing the serene and natural beauty of that moment by the sea.

photos.caption: Tech for Obama!
photos.captionKii: I remember seeing this fascinating piece of art, which looked like an intricate circuit board design, displayed on a canvas or large paper sheet. It was quite striking with its circular pattern divided into distinct blue and red halves, each filled with delicate lines, dots, and pathways. The way it was tacked up, possibly on a ceiling or high wall, made me look up and appreciate the blend of technology and artistic expression. The soft, ambient lighting really highlighted the clean lines and modern feel of the piece, making me wonder about its meaning or purpose.

photos.caption: Obama Tech Field Office in SOMA
photos.captionKii: I remember attending this bustling event, which looks like some kind of networking or industry gathering. The banner in the background clearly says 'TECHNOLOGY FIELD OFFICE', so it was probably related to tech. There were so many people, all chatting and mingling, creating a really lively atmosphere. I can see a mix of people in casual and smart-casual attire, some holding what look like drinks. The natural light streaming in from the windows gave the space a bright feel, even with the crowd. It was definitely a busy but interesting evening, surrounded by fellow professionals.

photos.caption: Abendessen mit Basti <3
photos.captionKii: I remember looking over this really charming, handwritten menu at a restaurant. The vibrant pink and black lettering, along with the little floral illustration next to 'Spaghetti,' made it feel so unique and personal. I can see options like 'Garlic Bread' and 'Rack Baby Back Ribs' under appetizers, and 'Spaghetti,' 'Penne Rigate,' and 'Shack Fish 'n Chips' for entrees. It was a cozy evening, and the warm, dim lighting created such a nice atmosphere as I decided what to order.

photos.caption: San Francisco punk zines from the 90s or "blogs in a box!" — at Prelinger Library
photos.captionKii: I was looking through this old cardboard box, which is just overflowing with my collection of zines and independent publications. It's a real treasure trove! I can see some cool ones right on top, like 'SKOOL COOL' with its playful black and white cartoons, and that vibrant red zine that reminds me of classic comics. There’s even one labeled 'Travel' in my handwriting, and another one hinting at 'art' with a red design. It was such a nostalgic trip to rediscover these unique, self-published works, each one a little piece of independent creativity.

photos.caption: Pretty sure this place has had the same bottle of spray since I've come here — Lone Palm
photos.captionKii: I remember holding up this bottle of 'Home Health Rose Water Body & Perfume Splash,' appreciating its light pink and white design and perhaps its scent. The background shows a lovely, blurry arrangement of orange-red roses, perfectly complementing the rose water theme, along with a wicker basket holding neatly folded white napkins or tissues. The dim, warm lighting in the room created a very cozy and intimate atmosphere at home that evening.

photos.caption: . @kidhack and the bird
photos.captionKii: I remember taking this picture of a man with a charming white cockatoo perched right on his shoulder. He's smiling widely, and the bird looks quite comfortable there, almost like a natural accessory. He's wearing a cool dark hoodie with some intricate patterns, which contrasts nicely with the bird's white feathers. The scene looks like it was captured in a dimly lit place, maybe indoors at night, which adds to the intimate and unique feel of the moment. It was such a fun and unexpected sight to see them together.

photos.caption: Chinese herbology, eat your medicine
photos.captionKii: I remember preparing this delicious homemade beet soup, capturing the vibrant red servings beautifully garnished with a dollop of white cream or yogurt and a fresh sprig of dill. Beside the glasses, there's a brown paper bag with slices of dark bread, likely rye. I also included a peek at my handwritten recipe note, listing ingredients like 'Beet, beets, cloves, cinnamon, salt, white' – it was a fun and tasty culinary experiment, and the warm green thermos nearby suggests I might have even made extra to keep warm.

photos.caption: "Older than Jesus" -@alastairmorse on traditional Chinese herbs.
photos.captionKii: I took this photo looking down into one of those fascinating traditional herbal medicine shops, specifically admiring the intricate wooden drawers. My feet, in my black Converse-style shoes, are visible on the left, showing I was right there, exploring. One of the top drawers is wide open, revealing a pile of light-colored, dried root slices or herbs in a paper bag. Below it, another drawer is partly open, showing smaller compartments filled with different types of darker, dried plant materials, possibly various herbs or seeds. The labels on the drawers, with both English and what look like Ch

photos.caption: Traditional Chinese "pharmacy"
photos.captionKii: I captured this intricate wall of wooden drawers, reminiscent of an old apothecary or traditional herbal medicine shop. Each small drawer, fitted with a brass label holder and a wooden knob, seems to house various compounds or herbs. I can clearly see some labels with both English and what appear to be Chinese characters, like 'Super Wind Camp' and 'Shin Jin Cao,' which really highlights the specialized and perhaps traditional nature of this collection. The warm, inviting glow of the room adds to the charm and historical feel of the space, making me wonder about all the remedies stored within.

photos.caption: Skyline for the Tahoe cabin
photos.captionKii: I captured a warm and lively moment during a dinner party with friends. We're all gathered around a wooden table, enjoying our meals—it looks like pasta with some green salads—and drinks, with bottles of beer and glasses visible. The lighting in the room is cozy, casting a warm glow, and everyone seems to be in good spirits.
