One of my friends on the left is mid-gesture, perhaps raising a glass for a toast, while another friend in a green hoodie gives a thumbs-up. It was a really nice evening spent together, full of good food and company.

photos.caption: The Ben Bieber — Because who doesn't want Bieber above their bed on their birthday? Ben does! (har)
photos.captionKii: I remember seeing this Justin Bieber poster, featuring a serious close-up of him at the top and a smaller full-body shot below, with his name prominently displayed. It was casually taped to a wall, possibly in a bedroom or a fan's space, showing a bit of wear and crinkling. The warm lighting in the room gives it a cozy, personal feel, really capturing a snapshot of pop culture from that time.

photos.caption: Hello tiny squab
photos.captionKii: I remember this exquisite dish I had, beautifully plated with such attention to detail. It looks like succulent glazed fowl, possibly quail, with a crispy element on top, nestled alongside vibrant green leaves, what appear to be roasted small vegetables, and bright, thinly sliced kumquats. The rich, dark sauce pooling around it just completes the sophisticated presentation, making it a truly memorable meal. I wanted to capture how artfully it was prepared.

I found this old, vintage-looking photo of myself and a friend, still nestled in what appears to be its original protective cardboard sleeve. The picture itself has a distinct greenish-yellow tint, which gives it a really cool, aged character, perhaps from the original film or how it's matured over time. I'm wearing my glasses and a plaid shirt, striking a pose with my hand on my hip, while my bearded friend stands next to me, holding what looks like a small camera or gadget. It's a fun memory to rediscover, capturing a casual moment from our past.

I captured this truly breathtaking view of the evening sky, painted in such a striking blend of deep purples and soft pinks. The clouds were subtly illuminated, creating these gentle, flowing patterns across the heavens. It was one of those moments where the sky just puts on a show, and I really wanted to preserve the serene and almost magical feeling of those vibrant colors fading into the twilight.

photos.caption: January!
photos.captionKii: I remember enjoying such a beautiful, sunny day at what looks like a vibrant city park, full of people relaxing on the expansive green lawn. The clear blue sky was absolutely perfect for an outdoor gathering, and it was great to see so many individuals and groups spread out on blankets, picnicking, chatting, and just soaking up the sun. I can spot a few palm trees adding to that quintessential California vibe, and some bicycles propped up, hinting at how people got there. The atmosphere was incredibly lively and relaxed, truly capturing a moment of collective enjoyment in the city.

photos.caption: 2012 coffee setup (thanks, mom!)
photos.captionKii: I captured my complete pour-over coffee brewing setup laid out on the kitchen counter, ready for a fresh cup. My Chemex is prominently placed on an Ozrri digital scale, with the coffee filters right next to it. I've also got my manual grinder standing by for the 'Esmeralda' coffee beans, and my trusty gooseneck kettle waiting to heat the water. It was a lovely, calm moment by the window, with some soft green foliage and hints of pink flowers visible outside, setting a peaceful tone for starting the day with this brewing ritual.

I captured this classic neon sign for the '500 CLUB' against the fading evening sky. The vibrant red glow of '500 CLUB' stands out boldly, accented by the iconic martini glass motifs—a large one at the very top and a smaller, greener one off to the side of the '0'. It really gives off that quintessential urban nightlife vibe. I remember thinking it was such a cool, old-school sign, perfect for a night out in the city.

I snapped a clear shot of the iconic BART logo, likely while I was at a station or on a train in the San Francisco Bay Area. The design, with its bold, intertwined 'b' and 'a' letters in dark and light blue, is instantly recognizable. Seeing this logo always brings to mind the experience of navigating the Bay Area via its public transit system, and this photo definitely marks a moment of travel or exploration during my day.

photos.caption: De-icing
photos.captionKii: I captured this abstract shot of water droplets clinging to a surface, likely a window or glass pane, on what must have been a wet day. The tiny beads of water, some larger rivulets, created a really interesting and intricate texture, almost like a natural mosaic. Through the blurred background, I can just make out some indistinct shapes and faint lights, adding to the moody, atmospheric feel. It's a simple yet captivating moment, capturing the essence of a rainy or frosty day.

photos.caption: Dayton, keeping it super real with the coffee. This is on Zagat's top 10 coffee places in the U.S.
photos.captionKii: I stopped by this inviting coffee shop and was immediately drawn to its warm, rustic atmosphere. The main counter, crafted from light wood, was quite eye-catching, and behind it, I could see two baristas busy at work with the impressive espresso machine and coffee grinders. The shelves were neatly stocked with various coffee-making accessories and products, adding to the specialized feel of the place. What really made the decor unique was the wooden canoe shell mounted high above the counter – it was such an unexpected and charming touch that I had to capture it. It felt like a perfect spot to

photos.caption: FORK IT OVER
photos.captionKii: I picked up a copy of the 'Dayton City Paper' and found the cover quite striking. It featured a cool graphic of a space shuttle, labeled 'United States,' soaring above some colorful, abstract shapes. The main headline, 'FORK IT OVER,' definitely caught my eye, and below it, there were intriguing mentions of '2011 Soundboard,' 'Steven Spielberg's War Horse,' and 'EPICUREAN 2.0.' It seems this was the December 20, 2011, issue, which I probably just got around to looking at on the 30th. There was even a note about a 'SPECIAL PULL-OUT SECTION!' and 'Destination Troy,' making it look like an intere

photos.caption: Grumpy corn / Happy buttter
photos.captionKii: I stumbled upon this absolutely wild and whimsical display inside what appeared to be a general store. The main attraction was a giant, anthropomorphic corn cob character, looking comically grumpy, wearing a straw hat and an orange and red checkered tie. Right beside him, a tall, slender yellow character with a huge, happy grin added to the quirky scene. They were set against a vibrant mural depicting a sunny sky and green fields, creating a really fun, almost cartoonish backdrop. Looking beyond them, I could faintly see the aisles of the store, filled with products, and above, some charming s

I spotted this impressive, life-sized statue of a giraffe, its classic brown spots contrasting nicely with its light body, standing tall against an overcast sky. In the background, peeking through some dry grasses, was another large statue—an elephant with its trunk curving upwards. The setting felt almost like a miniature safari, with artificial rock formations and greenery behind the animals, leading up to a building with a green roof. It was interesting to come across such a detailed and engaging display; it definitely caught my eye.

I found myself peering into the charming window display of 'The Bicycle Museum of America.' The grand, vintage-style lettering on the glass immediately caught my eye, highlighting the museum's name in bold blue and red. Inside, the window was filled with fascinating old bicycles, including what looks like a '1901 Wolff-American Lady's Bicycle' proudly on display, complete with an information card. A mannequin in period attire added to the historical ambiance, and some festive snowflake decorations hung from the ceiling, giving the whole scene a touch of holiday cheer. It was a delightful and u

photos.caption: Farm town Ohio
photos.captionKii: I captured this shot of what looks like an old-fashioned general store, proudly displaying 'BIRTS' in large, vibrant red letters on its facade. It was lightly snowing, adding a quiet, picturesque feel to the scene, with faint flakes visible in the air. The building itself has a rustic charm, with brown siding and a stepped brick design on the upper left. Outside, there's an old ice machine, and the entrance is adorned with simple holiday decorations, like red and white striped poles, suggesting we were still in the festive season. I was drawn to its classic small-town Americana feel, making it

photos.caption: Bowlin'
photos.captionKii: I captured this classic overhead shot of my feet clad in some wonderfully retro red, white, and blue bowling shoes with crisp white laces. Standing on the light wooden planks of a bowling lane, it's clear we were having some fun at the alley. It's always fun to get into the spirit with the right gear, and these shoes always put me in the mood to try for a strike!

photos.caption: Glitter bowlin' girls
photos.captionKii: I captured this sweet moment of two young girls, likely my relatives, enjoying their drinks at a table. The older girl on the left, wearing a cool cap and a sparkly blue top, is giving a cheerful smile directly at the camera. Beside her, the younger girl with blonde hair and a white headband is focused on her drink, sipping through a straw from a tall glass. The patterned carpet and other tables in the background suggest we were at a casual entertainment venue, possibly a bowling alley or a family recreation center. It was lovely to see them relaxed and having a good time together.

photos.caption: Post-Christmas Eyink bowling!
photos.captionKii: I captured this quirky sign for 'PLA-MOR DRIVE THRU -' on what looks like the side of a building, which immediately caught my eye. The wall is a light green corrugated metal, making the bold yellow and dark green lettering really stand out, especially with the eye-catching graphic of a bowling ball about to hit some pins. The 'DRIVE THRU' part is quite intriguing, suggesting it might be a unique type of establishment, perhaps for picking up bowling gear or something else related to recreation. It's a fun, retro-looking sign that feels like a classic local spot, and I wanted to remember this un

photos.caption: Kendall the wizard making science!
photos.captionKii: I snapped this delightful photo of a young girl, likely one of my younger relatives, during our Christmas Day celebration. She's absolutely beaming at the camera, wearing an adorable purple party hat adorned with stars, which really adds to the festive mood. She's holding a clear test tube, and in front of her on the wooden table, there are several more bottles or tubes containing colorful liquids. It looks like she's deeply engaged in a fun science or potion-making kit she received, probably one of her new Christmas gifts. It’s wonderful to capture these moments of pure joy and concentration

photos.caption: B the scientist making circuits
photos.captionKii: I captured this sweet, focused moment during our Christmas Day family celebration, where the kids were engrossed in playing with their new gifts. Here, I see a girl, likely one of my younger relatives, along with two boys, intently working on what appears to be a 'Snap Circuits' electronics kit spread across the wooden table. The girl in the blue and green striped sweater is carefully connecting the pieces, demonstrating great concentration. It's wonderful to see them so engaged in a hands-on activity, showcasing the joy of Christmas morning with new toys.

photos.caption: Only 21 of us this year
photos.captionKii: I captured this wonderful, lively scene of our Christmas Day family celebration. Everyone was gathered together, enjoying the festive atmosphere and the excitement of opening presents. There's a comfortable chaos with wrapping paper and gift boxes scattered around the floor, a clear sign of a successful gift exchange! My family members are relaxing, some sitting on chairs, others comfortably on the floor, while one of us is in the middle of unwrapping a gift. It's moments like these, surrounded by loved ones, that truly make Christmas special.

photos.caption: Merry Christmas, Love Ohio
photos.captionKii: I spotted this wonderfully festive giant rooster statue on Christmas Day! It was quite a sight, standing proudly on its pedestal with a vibrant green and red tinsel garland wrapped around it, complete with what look like white lights. The big white rooster with its striking red comb and wattle really popped against the partly cloudy sky. In the background, there's a classic red barn, which added to the charming rural scene. It was a delightful and unexpected piece of holiday cheer to capture.

photos.caption: Buckeye Brewing Company "Hippie IPA" Of course, Ohio. Of course.
photos.captionKii: I spotted these unique 'Hippie IPA' cans from Buckeye Brewing Company while I was browsing the craft beer selection at the pub. The design, featuring the Ohio state flag with its distinctive red, white, and blue pattern, really caught my eye and made them stand out among all the other options in the refrigerator. It was cool to see such a local touch on a craft beer, making me want to try this India Pale Ale.

I loved this moment in a pub, probably late evening, where I captured this fantastic display. The brightly lit refrigerator was packed with an incredible selection of craft beers, both in bottles and cans, making it tough to choose! What really caught my eye, though, was the bold, glowing sign above it declaring 'BEER IS FOOD.' It was such a humorous and fitting statement for the atmosphere. In the foreground, there were several glasses of dark beer lined up on the bar, perhaps a tasting flight, which made the whole scene feel like a true celebration of good brews. It really encapsulates the v

photos.caption: You know? NBD.
photos.captionKii: I captured this close-up of 'nbd.' (no big deal) scrawled in black marker on what looks like a light brown wooden door or panel. It was just one of those little quirky details I noticed, perhaps in a public space or someone's home, that made me smile. The casualness of the message, almost a shrug in text form, felt pretty amusing and worth snapping a picture of. There's a small screw visible in the bottom right, which just adds to the everyday, unassuming nature of the surface.

photos.caption: SF brought a pretty epic game to Berlin ping pong
photos.captionKii: I finally made it to the 'Berlin Style Ping Pong' place I saw advertised earlier, and it was exactly as quirky and lively as I'd hoped! The space itself felt like an art gallery combined with a social club, with vibrant red abstract art and large, striking eyes painted on the walls. Here, a crowd of us gathered around the ping pong table, with two players mid-game, one intensely focused with a red paddle. The atmosphere was buzzing with energy, laughter, and a bit of a festive spirit – I even spotted someone in the background wearing a Santa hat, making it feel like a unique evening out with f

I spotted this quirky chalkboard sign on a sidewalk late one evening, advertising 'BERLIN STYLE PING PONG.' The hand-drawn letters in chalk against the dark wooden board really caught my eye, pointing the way with an arrow. It was pretty dark out, with just enough ambient light to illuminate the sign and a bit of the storefront in the background, hinting at a lively place. It made me wonder what 'Berlin style' ping pong even means—definitely intriguing and something I'd want to check out! It felt like stumbling upon a hidden gem during a night out.

I captured this serene moment as three of us stood silhouetted against a breathtaking twilight sky. We were likely on a hilltop, overlooking the horizon where the last vestiges of sunset painted the clouds with soft oranges and pinks, fading into a deep, rich blue above. It was such a peaceful scene, just standing there, possibly chatting quietly or simply soaking in the beauty of the evening. I can just make out what looks like distant city lights on the right, adding to the feeling of being above it all, enjoying a quiet, reflective end to the day with good company.

photos.caption: Sutro the Sourdough, finally in bread dough form — What the below starter + flour (etc) looks like after 24 hours of rising in the refridgerator. Only a few hours more before it's time to make it into loaves (only to rise more)!
photos.captionKii: I was checking on the dough for my baking project, which I had shaped into a smooth, light brown ball with distinct ridges, almost like a small pumpkin. It's resting in a clear glass pie dish on my speckled kitchen counter. In the background, you can see my metal dish drying rack with an upside-down glass bowl, typical of my kitchen setup. This moment captures the dough in a later stage, likely after kneading and ready for its first or second rise, bringing me closer to the final baked treat.

photos.caption: Sutro the Sourdough, in the bread mix (!) — Finally adding Sutro to the flour that will make bread. (!)
photos.captionKii: I was starting another baking project, captured here with the initial mix of ingredients. In a clear glass bowl, a neat mound of white flour holds a well in its center, filled with a smooth, creamy liquid mixture, likely yeast and warm water or a starter. It’s the exciting first step of bringing ingredients together, setting the stage for what I hope will be a delicious bread or pastry. You can also spot my kitchen counter with its subtle speckles and a metal dish drying rack in the background, adding to the homely feel of this baking moment.

photos.caption: One of the best things in life: Sitting at the counter
photos.captionKii: I was so ready for a delicious meal at Pizzeria Delfina in San Francisco! Looking across the counter, I loved seeing all the fresh ingredients laid out, ready for pizza making. There were bowls of grated cheese, dark olives, rich red sauce, and what looked like savory mushrooms, plus a little pot of fresh rosemary. My place setting was already there, with the Pizzeria Delfina menu or napkin folded neatly, signifying the start of a wonderful dining experience. It was great to see the preparation in action and anticipate my pizza.

photos.caption:
Sutro the Sourdough, Day 2 — Attempting to make a sourdough starter in my kitchen. I figured while I live in San Francisco, I should capture some of the tasty yeast that floats in the air here to make traditional sourdough. 

This is day tw
photos.captionKii: I captured this image of a smooth, rounded ball of dough resting in a clear glass bowl, placed on my speckled kitchen countertop. The warm, inviting color of the dough suggests it's a wheat-based mixture, possibly for bread or a pastry. This moment, seeing the dough ready to rise or be worked, often feels like the exciting beginning of a new baking adventure.

photos.caption: Roasting magic
photos.captionKii: I was fascinated by this large, industrial coffee roasting machine, likely at a place like Four Barrel Coffee, given my recent visits. Seeing the roaster in action, with a worker tending to it, really highlighted the journey of the beans before they end up in my cup. The machinery looks so intricate and powerful, with all its belts and gears, and those sacks of beans in the background complete the scene of dedicated coffee crafting. It was an impressive sight to witness the scale of coffee production firsthand.

photos.caption: 4 months without SF coffee, doing catch up
photos.captionKii: I settled in with a beautifully crafted latte, adorned with delicate fern-like art, ready to dive into some reading or work. Beside my coffee, there's a special report on 'WOMEN AND WORK' with a striking cover, along with my trusty spiral notebook and pen. It felt like a perfect, focused moment of contemplation, perhaps reflecting on the theme of 'closing the gap' while enjoying my coffee.

photos.caption: Coffee class!
photos.captionKii: I captured this close-up of a menu or sign at what appears to be a coffee shop, 'Four Barrel Coffee'. It lists various coffee brewing methods like French Press, Chemex, Clever, V60, and Aeropress, which I found really interesting as it speaks to the art of coffee preparation. The bottom also states 'Fall 2011', marking the season of my visit. It was likely a moment where I was contemplating my coffee choice or appreciating the different options available for a perfect brew.

photos.caption: A Jack Kerouac Thanksgiving
photos.captionKii: Still enjoying the evening, I captured this shot of someone's legs in blue jeans, seated across from me at a table adorned with what looks like a spread of beautifully illustrated Tarot cards. A hand rests casually on their lap, and in the background, city lights or reflections can be seen through a window, adding to the cozy, atmospheric glow of the place. It felt like a perfect extension of our enjoyable outing, transitioning from dinner to a relaxed moment of conversation and perhaps some lighthearted card-reading.

photos.caption: "Investigate new possibilities with friends."
photos.captionKii: After a delightful meal, I opened my fortune cookie and captured this slip with its message: 'Investigate new possibilities with friends. Now is the time!' It was a fun and serendipitous end to the dinner, making me think about future adventures and connections.

photos.caption: Braaaainnnzzz
photos.captionKii: I snapped a close-up of this wonderfully plump and glossy mushroom, likely a shiitake, swimming in a rich, savory sauce. The way the light catches its smooth surface, highlighting the subtle patterns, and the glimpses of fresh green vegetables around it, made it look absolutely delicious. It was a highlight of the meal, and I wanted to remember how appetizing and well-prepared it was.

photos.caption: Missed you so much
photos.captionKii: After days of travel and sightseeing, it was a welcome change to slow down. I captured this moment of calm: a delicious latte with beautiful fern-like art, a glass of water, and my e-reader open to a page of text, all resting on a warm wooden table. It felt good to just sit, sip, and read, soaking in a quiet moment.

photos.caption: "Last time you were in California was over 3 months ago."
photos.captionKii: I captured this screenshot from my Foursquare app, celebrating a 'Nice check-in!' that earned me 8 points. It was interesting to see the app track my movements, noting that it had been over three months since my last time in California, which was at San Francisco International Airport. This check-in was my first time at the Amtrak - Sacramento Station (SAC), a new stop on our journey, and I even got bonus points for being the first of my friends to check in there. It’s always fun to see these little achievements pop up and mark significant moments in my travels.

photos.caption: Snowy desert, eastern Nevada
photos.captionKii: I captured this serene yet striking view of a snow-covered landscape from what looks like a train window. Sparse, frozen bushes dot the foreground, creating a delicate texture across the white ground, while in the distance, gentle mountains rise up, also dusted with snow under an overcast sky. It's a vast, quiet scene that feels miles away from any city, giving me a strong sense of the diverse terrains we're passing through on our journey. It was truly beautiful to watch the world go by, transformed by winter.

photos.caption: . @dannynewman came by to say hi in Denver! <3
photos.captionKii: I captured this cheerful close-up of my partner, beaming with a wide smile, wearing his cool sunglasses and a dark coat over a red shirt. The sunlight is bright, giving the shot a warm, almost nostalgic feel, and it looks like we were outdoors, possibly still exploring the city. I love how happy he looks here; it's moments like these that make our trips so special.

photos.caption: California Zephyr from Chicago, take 2
photos.captionKii: I captured this expansive view looking down a series of train tracks, stretching towards the distant city skyline under a bright, clear sky. The multiple tracks converge towards the heart of the city, where a cluster of tall buildings, including what looks like the distinctive Willis Tower, stands prominent on the horizon. There are railway signals and power lines visible, adding to the industrial yet dynamic feel of the scene, giving me a strong sense of the vast transit network connecting everything. It perfectly captures the experience of arriving at or departing from a major urban center b

photos.caption: Back in Chicago, starting from scratch
photos.captionKii: I captured this impressive interior shot of what looks like a grand train station, possibly Chicago's Union Station, with its towering, multi-paned windows at the top letting in a soft, diffused light. Below the windows, an ornate balcony adds to the architectural elegance. The focal point is the large archway, clearly marked with "TO ALL TRAINS," inviting travelers deeper into the station. You can see several people walking through the concourse below, giving a sense of the bustling activity within this magnificent building. It felt like I was stepping into a classic era of travel, a truly aw

photos.caption: "I'm coming home / via Chicago"
photos.captionKii: I captured this impressive panorama of a city skyline, with the sun casting a warm, golden glow across the buildings. The most prominent feature is the towering skyscraper with its distinctive twin antennas, which looks like the iconic Willis Tower, suggesting this is Chicago. In the foreground, I can see a railway line and some industrial structures, indicating I might have been on a train or an elevated platform, offering this unique vantage point. The sky above is a clear, serene blue, perfectly complementing the urban landscape.

photos.caption: Sax solos and soul covers
photos.captionKii: I captured this lively moment at a live music venue, where a fantastic band was performing. In the foreground, a saxophonist in a white shirt and tie was passionately playing, while behind him, a guitarist with gray hair kept the rhythm. The stage had a cool, unique vibe with string lights strung across the ceiling and the back wall decorated with an array of reflective CDs, adding to the atmosphere. It was such an enjoyable night, soaking in the energy of the live performance.

photos.caption: Quaintbridge, MA
photos.captionKii: I captured this beautiful autumn scene, with trees absolutely ablaze in vibrant yellow and red foliage along a quiet street. The sidewalks were completely covered in a thick carpet of fallen leaves, making it feel so quintessential fall. On the right, you can see a charming house with yellow siding and a white picket fence, while on the left, cars are parked along the road. The clear blue sky overhead truly made for a crisp and perfect autumn day.

photos.caption: Instead of candy the kids get their photo in front of a spooky background. Neat. :)
photos.captionKii: I snapped this fun group photo during our Halloween festivities, where all the kids gathered in their fantastic costumes in front of a bright green screen. We had quite the lineup: a mummy, a child with an interesting painted face, Buzz Lightyear, a smaller kid in camouflage, two ninjas, an elegant Egyptian princess, and another girl in a dark, flowy dress with red accents. An adult woman in a leopard print costume stands at the back, smiling. It was great to get everyone together for a studio-style shot, adding another memorable moment to our Halloween celebrations.

I captured this lively close-up of one of the girls during our Halloween celebrations. She's absolutely beaming, showing off a wide smile with bright eyes and those fun false eyelashes, all while sporting her stylish leopard-print fur-trimmed coat and beaded choker. In the background, you can see hints of our outdoor gathering with chairs, possibly a fire pit, and other party-goers, making it clear we were having a blast around the holiday.

photos.caption: All the kids before they demolish the neighborhood
photos.captionKii: I captured this fantastic group shot of all the kids lined up on the driveway, showcasing their incredible Halloween costumes, ready for a night of trick-or-treating! It was such a lively scene with a ninja, Buzz Lightyear, Spider-Man, a mummy, and an Egyptian-themed character among them, all holding their orange trick-or-treat buckets. In the background, some adults were gathered near the garage, enjoying the festive atmosphere, with pumpkins adding to the Halloween decor. It perfectly captured the excitement of our outdoor gathering.

I captured this fun Halloween moment featuring a woman dressed as a vampire, complete with a black gown, a high red-lined collar, and a choker, standing proudly with two costumed girls. One girl is a charming Egyptian princess, adorned with a golden beaded headpiece, an ornate necklace, and a white and teal gown, holding a small black and yellow bag. Beside her, another girl sports a stylish leopard-print fur-trimmed coat over a dark dress, also wearing a gold choker, with a white cloth gathered around her waist. It was a festive and playful scene, clearly ready for trick-or-treating or a Hall

I captured this serene twilight sky, with soft, elongated clouds stretching across the blue-pink gradient of the horizon. Below, the silhouettes of buildings and trees stood out against the fading light, suggesting the peaceful close of another day during my travels. It was a beautiful, quiet moment that felt very typical of the evenings I spent there in Germany, offering a tranquil end to my explorations.

photos.caption: Evil hair spike by Christoph
photos.captionKii: During one of my evenings in Germany, I captured this quiet moment. It shows the back of someone's head, with light brown hair, resting their hand near their forehead. The room is dimly lit, casting warm tones, and through the window in the background, you can just make out the blurry lights of a street at night. It was a peaceful and contemplative scene, perhaps after a long day of exploring, perfectly capturing the relaxed atmosphere of our evenings there.

During my trip to Germany, I came across this striking display of text on a wall, which was quite sobering. It features a quote from 1838, stating 'Die Kraft des Geldes liegt in den Händen des...' with the English translation clearly visible below: 'The power of money lies in the hands of the Jews.' It also mentions 'Catholic newspaper 1848 Violent.' This powerful and disturbing historical quote, likely part of an exhibition or museum, was a stark reminder of the deep-seated prejudices and historical context present in Germany's past. I wanted to capture it to remember this important, albeit u

Continuing my journey through Germany, I stumbled upon this fascinating open book, likely part of a museum exhibit, showcasing beautiful historical illustrations. The page displayed two intricately drawn fish, complete with scales and fins, in what appeared to be a scientific or natural history context. Below the illustrations, I could make out some German text, mentioning 'Gezeichnet auf Visiten Sr. Durchlaucht des Erprinzens von Sachsen Coburg,' which hints at its aristocratic patronage or origin. It was captivating to see such detailed artistry and historical documentation from an earlier e

photos.caption: Jewish communities in Germany, circa 1900
photos.captionKii: Continuing my trip to Germany, I encountered another fascinating map display. This one seemed to illustrate a network of towns and connections across a region, with prominent names like Nürnberg, Fürth, Würzburg, and Bayreuth clearly visible. Each town is marked by a red dot, and yellow lines intricately link them together, perhaps depicting historical trade routes, administrative divisions, or a transportation system. The map had a slightly aged, sepia-toned appearance, adding to its historical feel, and I was keen to capture this visual representation of Germany's geography and connectivity

Navigating through Berlin, I snapped a photo of this classic German street or subway sign, likely indicating directions at a station. The sign prominently features "Schönleinstraße" in bold black letters, along with other street names like "Schinkestr.", "Planufer", "Bürkerstr.", and "Böckstr.", all with directional arrows. I can also make out "Vittenau" (likely Wittenau) on the left side, confirming my location in Berlin. These signs are always so clear and helpful when exploring a new city, and I wanted to remember the distinct look of Berlin's urban wayfinding.

photos.caption: Mama
photos.captionKii: After a day of exploring, I found myself in a cozy, dimly lit place, likely a bar or pub, in Germany. This shot captures our table, illuminated by the warm glow of a single red candle, which added such a nice ambiance. Around it, we had several drinks—mostly beers, some nearly finished, and one glass of white wine. I can even make out the 'Svijany' logo on one of the beer glasses, a nice touch of local flavor. It was a perfect way to wind down and enjoy the evening, chatting and relaxing.

As the sun began to set during my trip to Germany, I found myself walking on what looked like an old airport runway. The light was just incredible, bathing everything in a warm, golden glow, and I wanted to capture this serene moment. In the foreground, there's a huge white 'R' painted on the asphalt, likely marking the runway, and I could see several other people enjoying the vast open space, some walking, some on bicycles or even a wheelchair. It was such a unique experience to be able to just stroll across a former airfield, especially with that beautiful sunset creating such a peaceful end

During my trip to Germany, I came across this fascinating large banner displaying what looks like an intricate historical map or urban planning diagram, likely of Berlin. The sepia-toned background contrasts with structures highlighted in light blue and some areas in red, illustrating the city's layout or a proposed grand vision. I can spot recognizable landmarks like the Reichstag and the Brandenburg Gate, along with various street names and districts written in German. It was really interesting to see such a detailed visual representation of the city's past or planned development, giving me

The modern, light-colored facade prominently displayed its name in black lettering, with large windows revealing the interior. A person sat casually on a wooden bench just outside the entrance, contributing to the urban scene. To the right, an entrance to what appeared to be a parking area or driveway was visible, marked with a red 'Feuerwehr-Zufahrt' (Fire Department Access) sign, hinting at the building's multi-functional nature. It was interesting to see the ph

During my trip, I stumbled upon this interesting flyer displayed on what appeared to be a community board, perhaps in a co-working space like Betahaus, indicated by some text in the background. The white paper, fastened with two small white clothespins to a pink string, features a prominent QR code. photos.caption: Maschinenraum, Betahaus
photos.captionKii: I peeked into a 'Maschinenraum' or 'Machine Room' during my trip, capturing this image of the heavy white door with its distinct porthole window. The blue sign with white lettering clearly indicated the room's purpose. Through the porthole and the slightly ajar door, I could glimpse a fascinating workshop filled with various industrial tools and machinery, suggesting a place of hands-on work and technical activity. It was another interesting discovery, highlighting the industrial and technical side of what I was exploring in Germany.

photos.caption: SPD bird house — Möritzplatz
photos.captionKii: While exploring Germany, I came across this intriguing red, house-shaped structure mounted on a pole, set against a bright blue sky. It had prominent white letters spelling "SPD" on one side and "Berlin verstehen." on another, suggesting it was related to local politics or a clever piece of urban art in Berlin. The roof seemed to be made of a golden material, and a few blue and yellow tubes poked out from the top, adding to its whimsical appearance. It was a neat, unexpected detail that offered a glimpse into the local culture and public messaging.

I captured this image of a TACANCHECKCOURSE sign during my trip. The sign, which stood out prominently in a grassy field under a slightly cloudy sky, displayed technical information in bright yellow letters on a dark background. It read 'TOF (CH88) 314 134', 'TACANCHECKCOURSE', and 'DIST. TO ANT .6 MILE.' Recognizing 'TACAN' as a navigation system primarily used by military aircraft, this sign clearly indicated that I was near an airfield or some kind of aviation facility. It was an interesting piece of specific aviation infrastructure to stumble upon, adding another layer to my explorations r

photos.caption: "Piraten im Dialog"
photos.captionKii: I continued to delve into the local news during my trip, capturing another shot of the 'Frankfurter Allgemeine Zeitung' newspaper from September 20, 2011. This page features a prominent article titled 'Piraten im Dialog' (Pirates in Dialogue), likely discussing the German Pirate Party. It's quite interesting to see it accompanied by a photo that appears to be from a football match, which shows the diverse range of content presented in German newspapers. These quiet moments of reading helped me feel more connected to the current events and culture during my time in Germany.

photos.caption: Pirate coverage — "Gut gegen Piraten"
photos.captionKii: I was looking at the 'Frankfurter Allgemeine Zeitung' newspaper, which I picked up on September 20, 2011. It's always interesting to see the local news when traveling, and this German paper had some prominent headlines. I could see an article about 'Obama: Defizit ausbauen' (Obama: expanding the deficit) and another, quite charming one, titled 'Gut gegen Piraten' (Good against pirates), featuring Pippi Longstocking with treasure chests. Also, there was a headline about 'Merkel: We will continue government work'. It was a quiet moment, catching up on current events and popular culture during my

photos.caption: Indecisively adding and removing the beer coaster from my Kölsh glass. #koelschconfusion
photos.captionKii: After a day of exploring, I settled down to enjoy a refreshing glass of Früh Kölsch beer. It was served in this classic tall, slender glass, topped with a nice, frothy head. I love seeing the 'FRÜH KÖLSCH' logo printed clearly on the glass, letting me know I was enjoying a true local specialty from Cologne, Germany. The coaster underneath also had some brewery details, adding to the authentic experience. It was a perfect way to relax and reflect on everything I had seen and learned, embracing the local culture during my visit.

photos.caption: Columbus simulator
photos.captionKii: I captured this sign for the European Astronaut Centre and the Columbus Simulator during my visit. It was really interesting to see the emblem for the European Space Agency (ESA) prominently displayed, alongside the text indicating the purpose of the facility. The Columbus module is such an important part of the International Space Station, and knowing I was near a simulator for it made the whole experience feel even more real and immersive. It added another layer to understanding the incredible work done in space exploration and astronaut training, complementing the mission control room I saw

photos.caption: !! — ISS operations at ESA, Cologne
photos.captionKii: I was absolutely fascinated by this view inside what looks like a mission control room. It felt incredibly high-tech and important, with so many screens displaying live data, satellite feeds, and complex information. I could clearly see a large screen showing a satellite in space, another perhaps an astronaut at work, while the smaller monitors showcased maps, star patterns, and intricate schematics. The DLR logo on one of the top left screens caught my eye, indicating it was the German Aerospace Center. It was awe-inspiring to imagine all the critical work happening here, monitoring space mis

photos.caption: Nach Köln für Weltraum und Flugzeuge.
photos.captionKii: After my ride, I paused to capture a full view of my vibrant pink road bike, parked here on the tiled floor near a set of escalators. I really love how the bright pink frame stands out against the more muted, functional tones of the indoor space, with its glass and metal railings. It looks like it's patiently waiting, perhaps for its next journey, or maybe I was bringing it inside or taking it on public transport. I remember draping a light-colored cloth over the top tube, probably to protect it or for quick access after a long ride. It was a nice moment to appreciate my ride and my bike befor

photos.caption: Hot pink bike. Juhuu!! — It needs a name.
photos.captionKii: I was out enjoying a bike ride, looking down at the handlebars as I pedaled along. My hand is visible on the left side, gripping the white-taped handlebars, and the vibrant pink frame of my bike stands out against the blurred texture of the ground passing beneath me. It was one of those moments where the feeling of movement and the simple joy of cycling were all that mattered, and I wanted to capture that perspective.

I was really drawn to the warm, inviting glow of this double light fixture against the distinct patterned wallpaper. The subtle illumination highlighted the intricate, almost retro design of the wallpaper, making the whole corner feel cozy and a bit mysterious. It's one of those small details that really sets the mood in a room, and I wanted to capture that particular ambiance.

photos.caption: co.up robot!
photos.captionKii: I spotted this absolutely delightful and clever piece of everyday art! Someone drew a little chalk robot or character on a dark wall, using a regular light switch as its head. The simple square switch became the face, and chalk lines formed its body, arms flexing with fists, and little legs. It instantly brought a smile to my face, turning something so mundane into a playful moment. It's these small, creative touches that really make a place feel unique and fun.

photos.caption: co.up workspace, Gernot talks about Soundcloud at up.front.ug
photos.captionKii: I found myself at an engaging evening event, possibly a lecture or a community talk, in a room filled with people. The dim lighting, mostly from wall-mounted fixtures and a ceiling light, created an intimate atmosphere as everyone focused on the speaker, whose back is to the camera on the right. There was also a screen visible in the background, likely displaying presentation slides. It was a classic scene of collective learning or discussion, with many attendees intently watching and listening, some with laptops open, suggesting a serious yet casual gathering.

photos.caption: Oh dear
photos.captionKii: I was checking out the drink aisle, and these bottles of BIONADE really caught my eye. It's a popular German organic soft drink, and they had so many interesting flavors like Holunder (elderflower), Kräuter (herbal), and Quitte (quince) all lined up. The price tag for EUR 1,99 even had the catchy slogan 'BIO? LOGISCH!' meaning 'Organic? Logical!' It's always fun to discover unique local products like this while I'm exploring new places.

photos.caption: Happy Heiko in the spy tower!
photos.captionKii: I found myself in a really raw and artistic urban space, likely some kind of underpass or abandoned structure, where the concrete walls were completely covered in layers of vibrant graffiti. It was such a striking backdrop! I managed to capture a person, seen from behind, mid-descent down a flight of concrete stairs, their arms thrown up in a playful 'rock on' gesture. The motion blur really emphasizes the dynamic energy of the moment as they moved through this unique, gritty environment. It felt like a hidden world full of stories and spontaneous art.

photos.caption: "We're probably going to get struck by lightning sitting on this runway"
photos.captionKii: Under a dramatically cloudy sky, I captured this moment of a man intently focused on his phone, which glowed brightly in his hands. It looked like he was either taking a photo, recording something, or perhaps sharing a special moment online, bathed in the phone's light. Next to us, a bottle, possibly champagne given its shape and foil, was visible on the ground, hinting at a quiet celebration or a special occasion as the evening drew in. The distant lights from buildings on the horizon added to the serene, yet somewhat expectant, atmosphere of the scene, creating a sense of quiet anticipation.

photos.caption: Zug Zug Zug!
photos.captionKii: As my European journey continued, I found myself waiting on a platform, capturing this iconic bright yellow train with its doors wide open, inviting passengers aboard. The illuminated interior of the subway car, along with the distinct number '013-2' on its side, made for a quintessential urban transit scene. It's moments like these, using the efficient local public transportation, that really make you feel immersed in the daily rhythm of a European city.

I spotted this distinctive logo for 'Berliner Kindl,' a local German beer brand, which features a charming cartoon child with brown hair peeking out from behind a gray mug. The design, set against a red circle and framed by a decorative, hops-like pattern, really caught my eye. Encounters with these unique local brands are always a fun part of exploring European culture.

photos.caption: Ben Ward's favorite cafe in Berlin... ;)
photos.captionKii: During my European journey, I stumbled upon what looked like a fantastic board game café or store, brimming with countless games. The shelves lining the walls were absolutely packed from floor to ceiling with colorful boxes, showcasing a massive collection. In the foreground, one of the wooden tables had a couple of game boxes, with 'GO' clearly visible on the top one, ready for play. Other patrons were scattered around, with some enjoying games at tables on the left and another person browsing the extensive collection in the background. The colorful chairs, a mix of orange and green, added to

photos.caption: Data privacy protest, Unter den Linden
photos.captionKii: Walking through what looked like a major street in a European city, I unexpectedly encountered a vibrant protest or demonstration. A large crowd of people filled the street, many holding signs and banners, some of which had German text like 'FREIHEIT, DATEN, BEGEHR' (Freedom, Data, Desire). What really caught my eye were the numerous large, bright red balloons floating high above the crowd, adding a striking visual element to the scene. Some individuals were even wearing distinctive Guy Fawkes masks, a symbol often associated with online activism and protest. In the background, I could see a g

photos.caption: "For starting camp fires or burning cars." - Heiko
photos.captionKii: I stopped in a local store, perhaps a German one given the packaging, and picked up some 'Anzünder' – firelighters! My hand is holding the black and fiery-looking box, which clearly states '32x' and has a partially visible price tag of '1.99'. It's a practical purchase, likely for an evening fire or a barbecue, showcasing another side of my European journey beyond just sightseeing, as I gather supplies from local shops.

photos.caption: Another escape tunnel, looking east to west
photos.captionKii: I continued my exploration of historical sites and came across this poignant outdoor marker. A series of rectangular metal plates were set into the grass, leading the way, with the closest one clearly indicating 'Fluchttunnel 'Tunnel 57' 1964'. It was a powerful reminder of the efforts made during a divided time. In the distance, I could see a yellow tram passing by, a stark contrast between the city's modern life and the deep history commemorated here. This site truly added another layer to understanding the complex past I've been encountering during my European journey.

photos.caption: Stasi tunnel, Bernauerstr.
photos.captionKii: I paused to capture this thought-provoking marker on the grass, a series of metal plates leading the way. The most prominent one clearly reads 'Stasi Tunnel' in a rustic, possibly weathered, metal lettering. It was a stark reminder of the historical significance of this location, and truly added another layer of depth to my European journey, exploring the complex past of the continent.

photos.caption: Mauer jungle
photos.captionKii: I found myself reflecting on the somber history of the region as I captured this scene. A pile of weathered concrete pipes lay discarded on the grass, looking like silent relics of a past era, with some plants even finding a home within their hollows. Behind them, a rough concrete wall, perhaps a remnant of an old structure, stood partially obscured by slender trees reaching towards the sky. The overall atmosphere was a bit melancholic but deeply historical, reinforcing the profound stories etched into the landscape during my European journey. It felt like another tangible piece of the complex

photos.caption: Pretty incredible to see this after studying art and history for so long.
photos.captionKii: I was absolutely captivated by the sheer scale and ancient grandeur of these majestic columns. Bathed in the warm glow of what felt like late afternoon sun, the intricate details of the weathered stone were brought to life, casting long, dramatic shadows against the perfectly clear blue sky. It was awe-inspiring to stand among such historical remnants, feeling the weight of centuries of history in a single glance. I wanted to capture the powerful presence of these architectural wonders, a true highlight of my European journey.

photos.caption: View from one of Cohen's old haunts
photos.captionKii: I captured this breathtaking view of a picturesque coastal town at dusk or dawn, nestled beautifully into a hillside. The white buildings with their warm, red-tiled roofs cascade down towards the tranquil harbor, where I could just make out boats gently resting. On the right, a distinctive clock tower stands tall, adding a classic touch to the scene. In the foreground, I loved the pop of color from the vibrant pink bougainvillea and the playful blue fence, framing this idyllic Mediterranean vista. It felt so serene and captured the essence of my European journey.

photos.caption: Swim. Sea. Sunset.
photos.captionKii: I captured this serene moment, propping my feet up at the edge of the crystal-clear water, perhaps by a small pier or natural rock shelf. The water was so inviting, with beautiful shades of blue and green, reflecting the bright sunlight. In the background, a majestic golden-brown rocky cliff face rose up, creating a dramatic backdrop to the calm sea. I loved the feeling of absolute relaxation, with my toes, freshly painted red, almost touching the cool water. It was a perfect pause during my journey, soaking in the tranquility and natural beauty of the European coast.

I captured this serene landscape from inside a moving vehicle, likely a train, looking out at the expansive European countryside. The golden, sun-baked fields stretched out under a clear blue sky, gently rolling towards the distant, hazy hills. A few scattered trees dotted the dry, grassy terrain, adding a sense of quiet solitude to the vast open space. I noticed the subtle reflections on the window, which hinted at the interior of the vehicle and reinforced the feeling of being on a journey, passing through these tranquil scenes and soaking in the beauty of the landscape.

photos.caption: From Thessaloniki to Athens
photos.captionKii: I captured this view from what looks like a train platform, pausing under the concrete shelter on a bright, sunny day. I loved the sense of openness, looking out across the vast fields towards the distant hills. It felt like a classic European rural scene, emphasizing the journey between places. I noticed the tracks stretching into the distance and even a simple bench and water tap on the platform, giving it a quiet, unhurried atmosphere. It made me reflect on the slow pace of travel and the beautiful landscapes you encounter along the way.

photos.caption: Drying peppers
photos.captionKii: I captured this charming scene of a traditional stone house on a bright, sunny day. What really caught my eye were the long strings of vibrant red peppers hanging to dry on the balcony railing – it just looked so authentic and picturesque. Below, everyday life was also on display with clothes flapping on a line. The old stone walls, the quaint roof, and even the antenna on top all contributed to a feeling of being in a classic European village. I wanted to remember this glimpse into a simple, beautiful way of life.

photos.caption: "He who wants the world to remain as it is doesn't want it to remain at all." <3 — East Side Gallery
photos.captionKii: I was really struck by this poignant piece of art on the Berlin Wall, captured at night under the artificial lights. The main artwork features a stark, leafless tree, framed by the powerful quote in both German and English: 'HE WHO Wants THE WORLD TO REMAIN AS IT IS DOESN'T WANT IT TO REMAIN AT ALL.' Below it, I noticed the names 'ERICH FRIED' and 'M. ELISA BUDZINSKI BERLIN 1990,' which adds a layer of artistic and historical context to the scene. The raw graffiti outside the main framed section also caught my eye, highlighting the ever-evolving nature of this historic landmark. It felt profou

photos.caption: Took this same picture in 1998 — East Side Gallery
photos.captionKii: I captured this evocative section of the Berlin Wall at night, feeling the weight of its history. The prominent mural with the large 'BERLIN' text, flanked by 'TOKYO' and abstract designs like barbed wire and floating spheres, really stood out against the dark sky. I also noticed the years '1989 2009' painted near the bottom, which adds to the historical significance of this artistic expression. The mix of structured art and more spontaneous graffiti on the adjacent sections created a powerful visual statement.

photos.caption: Basti!
photos.captionKii: I captured this moment of a friend relaxing on the sofa, holding a drink, during a cozy evening. The lighting was quite dim, giving the whole room a warm, intimate feel, with those red candles on the candelabra in the background adding to the ambiance. He looked right at me, and I just wanted to remember that relaxed, easygoing atmosphere we had together.

photos.caption: "Stolperstein" or stumbling block are all over the city as small memorials to those killed in camps during WW2. It reads, "Here lived Leo Goldstein born 1843. Deported 1942. Murdered in Auschwitz."
photos.captionKii: I stumbled upon this incredibly moving 'Stolperstein' embedded in the cobblestones, a small brass plaque commemorating Leo Goldstein. Seeing his name, birth year (1925), and the tragic words 'DEPORTIERT 1943 ERMORDET IN AUSCHWITZ' (Deported 1943 Murdered in Auschwitz) really stopped me in my tracks. It's such a powerful and poignant way to remember individual victims of the Holocaust right where they lived. The simplicity of it, yet the profound weight of history it carries, made a lasting impression.

I was really drawn to the distinct architecture of this building as the light began to fade. The way the concrete facade caught the last bit of daylight, contrasting with the vibrant green glow from the lower windows and the warm yellow light from the upper ones, was truly captivating. It felt like the building itself was coming alive with its own internal light, creating such a cool visual against the soft blue sky. I particularly liked the structured lines and the way the different colored lights added a touch of modern artistry to the urban landscape.

photos.caption: Mmmmmm
photos.captionKii: I was really struck by the dramatic, almost stark silhouette of this building against the sky. The dark, triangular structure creates such a bold, minimalist statement, pointing directly upwards towards the blue expanse. I loved how the scattered clouds provided a soft contrast to its sharp lines, making it feel both grounded and aspirational. It was a moment of appreciating truly striking modernist design.

photos.caption: Also the iTunes philosophy
photos.captionKii: I remember seeing this poster and immediately connecting with its powerful message. The bold red text, 'MOVE FAST AND BREAK THINGS,' was a familiar and impactful motto in the tech world, encapsulating a philosophy of rapid innovation and challenging existing paradigms. I also noted the smaller print at the bottom, crediting 'THE PROFESSOR ANALOGUE RESEARCH LABORATORY,' which gave it a slightly enigmatic and intriguing origin. It was a thought-provoking piece, simply taped to a white wall, yet it perfectly captured a certain ethos I appreciated.

photos.caption: Soundcloud party at 1 a.m.

I was truly mesmerized by the forest at the Chaos Communication Camp, especially at night when the trees came alive with these incredible, shifting lights. The canopy and branches were bathed in a kaleidoscope of colors – rich purples, bright greens, warm yellows, and hints of red – creating such an otherworldly and magical glow against the dark sky. It really highlighted how the camp transformed every corner, even the natural surroundings, into an artistic spectacle, making the nighttime strolls so enchanting.

I remember walking through the camp at night and being captivated by these trees, their trunks and leaves dramatically lit up with vibrant, shifting colors. There were brilliant blues, purples, and greens washing over them, creating such an ethereal and almost magical atmosphere in the darkness. It was another one of those moments that really showcased the creative lighting and artistic installations scattered throughout the Chaos Communication Camp, transforming the natural environment into something truly enchanting after sunset.

I remember walking around the Chaos Communication Camp at dusk and being captivated by how they transformed everyday objects with light. I remember holding up my official NASA credentials while riding what felt like a shuttle or bus, likely on our way to or from another part of the Kennedy Space Center. The other was a 'NASA SPECIAL GUEST' badge. It was a tangible reminder of our unique access and experience at such an incredible place, and I wanted to capture it as part of the journey.

I remember spotting this official-looking black sign mounted on a light-colored wall, humorously declaring 'BICYCLES PROHIBITED IN VAB' with a clear no-bicycles icon. I captured it because it was one of those quirky, specific rules that made the whole experience feel even more authentic and unique.

Above it all, the sky was a brilliant, clear blue, making the vastness of the ocean feel even more awe-inspiring. It was truly a moment to appreciate the raw power and beauty of nature.

photos.caption: A list created with @meghantosh about what I want to be/have/do/something when I'm old. Made over a nice bottle of wine.
photos.captionKii: I remember writing down this list of qualities and aspirations. It was a really personal moment, capturing some of the things I valued or aimed for at the time. I wrote 'Understated,' 'Creative,' and 'Californian' as descriptors, and then more active goals like 'Drink good wine' and 'Live in the future.' I even recall a line that looks like '"A Fund"', which makes me wonder if I was thinking about some sort of investment or project. It was clearly a moment of reflection and setting intentions for myself.

photos.caption: San Francisco. Not smug. Just better.
photos.captionKii: I remember seeing this bold statement painted on a white wall, right next to a fence enclosing a green field that looks like a park or school sports area. The words, 'SAN FRANCISCO. NOT SMUG. JUST BETTER.' really caught my eye. It's such a classic San Francisco sentiment, full of local pride, and I just had to capture it. The little weeds growing at the base of the wall added a bit of natural contrast to the urban grit, and the clear sky above completed the quintessential city scene.

I remember being really struck by the incredibly ornate and reflective walls in this room. They were covered in such a vibrant gold and brown swirling pattern, almost like a luxurious marbling effect, and the reflective surface made the whole space feel quite grand. I can see a door in the reflection, blending seamlessly into the elaborate design. On the left, there was a small, colorful arrangement of red and white flowers and greenery sitting on a ledge, providing a lovely contrast to the metallic sheen of the walls. It was such a visually captivating space, making me wonder where I was at t

photos.caption: Miike Snow
photos.captionKii: I remember being right in the thick of it at a concert, surrounded by fellow music lovers. The whole venue was alive with these incredible light beams, some glowing in warm oranges and browns, others cutting through the smoky air in cool blues and greens. I was looking up towards the stage, past the silhouettes of the crowd, feeling completely immersed in the energy. It was one of those moments where the music and the visuals just created a powerful, unforgettable atmosphere. This must have been the Hot Chip concert at Fox Oakland on April 16th, as I remember it being a really vibrant show.

I remember seeing the iconic Fox Oakland theater lit up so beautifully at night! The vibrant red neon sign for 'FOX OAKLAND' stood tall against the dark sky, and the marquee below was just as dazzling with its colorful lights. I can clearly see it listed 'APRIL 14 & 15 - ATOMS FOR PEACE' and '16 - HOT CHIP.' It was such a cool sight to take in before heading inside for the show, or perhaps just passing by and admiring the historic venue, especially given the concert I attended around this time.

I remember this incredible moment from a concert I attended, looking down from what felt like a balcony seat. The stage was absolutely mesmerizing, bathed in these vibrant green and white lights that were constantly shifting and creating cool, geometric patterns. Even from up here, I could make out the musicians and their instruments, a blur of energy. Below, the crowd was a sea of dark shapes, with little dots of light from phones or glow sticks adding to the atmosphere. It was such a powerful experience, completely immersed in the live music and the visual spectacle.

I remember capturing this iconic view of the San Francisco skyline, perfectly framed through what looks like a porthole or a unique window. The sky looks clear and bright, and the warm light suggests it might have been late afternoon or early evening. It’s such a classic perspective of the city, making me wonder if I was on a boat, a ferry, or even at a un

photos.caption: Bonestorm

It's funny how these early Foursquare alerts were such a big part of how we stayed connected and knew what everyone was up to. The background of my phone screen looks like some textured green foliage, and the 'AT&T 3G' signal indicator really takes me back to those days when Foursquare was a cutting-edge way to keep up with friends' late-night activities.

Looking at my phone's home screen, I can also see that I had a couple of new messages and a significant number of missed calls on the Phone app, indicating it was likely a busy social night or I was catching up on things. It truly takes me back to the early

This was probably me taking a moment to myself, enjoying a drink and snack while processing everything and getting back into a routine, even if it meant a bit of work or reading. photos.caption: We be sleeping, yo — SF Embassy got a bit rowdy :)
photos.captionKii: I remember needing some serious rest after the intense energy of SXSW. I must have been so exhausted that I put this handwritten 'ZZZ... (we be sleeping yo)' sign on the door. It was a funny, casual way to make sure no one disturbed my much-needed recovery sleep after all the late nights and energetic performances. The little '6dooneys.com' logo on the side also adds a touch of personality to this silly but effective 'do not disturb' note, perfectly capturing my desire to just crash after such a fun, busy event.

I remember being right there, enjoying another incredible performance at SXSW. This band was so energetic! I loved watching the frontman, dressed in that stylish light grey suit, black hat, and sunglasses, really getting into it and dancing across the stage with so much swagger. His moves were captivating. In the back, the drummer was totally focused, keeping the beat going strong, and you can see a few other band members in the shadows. You can really feel the raw, live energy of the festival in this shot, and it brings back the excitement of discovering new music and experiencing these dynam

I remember being right in the thick of it, surrounded by so many people with their hands up, really getting into the music and the vibe. It was such an electric atmosphere, with everyone cheering and holding up their drinks. I can see a friend with curly hair and a beard raising his hand on the right, and another person with glasses looking towards me in the middle, looking a bit overwhelmed but still part of the action. The energy was just incredible, and it perfectly captured the spirit of the festival, feeling completely immersed in the moment with everyone around me.

photos.caption: Muse — at Stubb's
photos.captionKii: I remember this incredible moment, standing in the dark with the crowd, completely captivated by the band on stage. The bright blue stage lights were so dramatic, illuminating the musicians and creating such a cool, energetic atmosphere. It looked like everyone around me was just as into it, with so many people holding up their phones to capture the energy of the performance. You could really feel the buzz of the festival, especially at night when the lights just made everything pop and added to the immersive experience.

I remember seeing this person from behind, wearing a denim jacket and light-colored pants, with a bright yellow bag slung over their back that humorously declared 'FULL HALF.' We were clearly still on our RV trip, as I could spot the familiar white bus in the background along with other people standing around. It was another one of those casual, unscripted moments that made the journey so memorable, just stopping somewhere along the way and taking in the scene with fellow travelers.

photos.caption: Holy Fuck

The energy was palpable, and you could just feel the buzz of the festival all around us. It was such a unique experience to catch acts in these intimate tent venues, surrounded by fellow music lovers.

I remember seeing this fun, hand-written sign taped to the side of our white RV during the day, which read 'FREE HIGH FIVES' with an arrow pointing towards the open door. It was such a cheerful and welcoming touch! Through the doorway, I could just make out a glimpse of someone inside, perhaps getting ready to offer those high fives, and also noticed a cool piece of wall art depicting a woman with sunglasses, adding to the laid-back vibe of our trip.

I remember seeing this large white RV pulled over on the side of the road late at night. There was a person standing off to the right, mostly obscured by the shadows, looking out into the night, adding to the feeling of being in a quiet, perhaps remote, spot. You can just barely see the tops of some trees along the bottom, grounding the scene, but my focus was entirely on the cheerful pattern the lights made against the bright expanse of the sky. It was a simple but lovely sight that caught my eye while exploring.

I remember seeing this unique spot, possibly called Hoagie's, with its distinct outdoor bar vibe. I remember stopping by this place, likely a bar or restaurant, and seeing this cool neon 'OPEN' sign shining through the window. The bright blue cowboy hat above the glowing orange 'OPEN' really caught my eye, giving the place a distinct Western vibe. Reflected in the glass, I can see hints of the street outside with cars, suggesting it was evening. photos.caption: Foursquare after party at Blind Pig — You know they're awesome when they thrown their after party at an all-American dive bar with some stoner cover band playing karaoke hits and everyone's drinking cheap beer. Hell yea!
photos.captionKii: I remember the lively energy of this crowded night out. The place was absolutely packed with people, and it captures the dense, spirited atmosphere of the evening. To her right, a man in a plaid shirt is looking somewhat directly at the camera, standing amidst the throng. Everyone seemed to be enjoying themselves, surrounded by the buzz of conversation and music, making for a truly memorable night.

I remember this lively night out, likely at a bar or club, judging by the dim lighting and the dense crowd. My friend, in a light beige long-sleeve shirt with a yellow undershirt peeking out, is caught mid-exclamation, smiling widely at the camera with one arm raised in the air. He's clearly energized and enjoying the moment. To his right, a woman in a blue top is holding a can of Coors Light, and in the foreground, another friend in a grey hoodie is looking towards the excitement. It was definitely a fun, spirited evening, full of good company and a great vibe.

photos.caption: Check it out, we're on the cover!

It was a fun little discovery, especially after we had seen those clever analog social media notes earlier. The bar was still lively around us, with neon signs glowing in the dim light, and I can even see a hand holding a camera in the foreground, suggesting another friend was capturing the moment too.

I remember spotting this amusing 'Analog Twitter' and 'Analog Twitpic' note taped to a window, likely in a cafe or bar. It was such a witty and unexpected observation, a perfect blend of modern social media concepts appl

I remember coming across this rather grim and unusual sight while walking. There was a dead fish lying on the wet, dark pavement, right next to a crumpled red plastic bag. It was a stark and unexpected moment, making me wonder about the story behind it, and I felt compelled to photograph this strange street discovery.

I remember spotting this playful graffiti while I was out and about. Someone had spray-painted the letters 'ABC' in a bright blue onto a lush green hedge. It stood out so much against the natural green and brown foliage. It looked like a fun, unexpected piece of street art or just someone having a bit of fun. I thought it was an interesting contrast, so I snapped a quick photo to remember it.

I remember the buzzing excitement outside the venue for Lady Gaga's Monster Ball concert. The marquee was brightly lit, proudly announcing "VIRGIN MOBILE PRESENTS THE MONSTER BALL FEATURING LADY GAGA, KID CUDI & SEMI PRECIOUS WEAPONS," along with the dates December 13th and 14th. The crowd was already thick, with people raising their arms and snapping photos, all eager to get inside or just soaking in the atmosphere. It was a cool night, and the energy was palpable, making it a memorable moment before the show began.

The title, "HUNT MOOSE," was clearly visible in large white letters, immediately telling me what the game was about. Below the title, there was a detailed image of a large moose with impressive antlers, standing in what looked like a forest or grassy field. It captured the essence of a wilderness hunting game, and I found it interesting enough to snap a photo.

photos.caption: So smug

It's an Oregon plate that reads "TWEED SALMON," which instantly caught my eye. I always find it fascinating to see what clever or quirky things people put on their plates, and this one definitely stood out as an interesting urban detail I noticed while I was out.

I remember seeing this striking warning sign while I was out. It featured a stark black silhouette of a person being struck by lightning, set against a clean white background. The intense warning symbol really caught my eye, with the zigzag lightning bolt clearly indicating danger. The way the sign was framed, with part of a dark, textured surface visible on the right, added to the overall visual, making it stand out as an interesting urban detail I wanted to capture.

I remember capturing this twilight scene, looking up a street as the sky transitioned from day to night. The silhouette of the city buildings and hills against the soft gradient of the sky was really striking. In the distance, I could clearly see the distinct shape of the Sutro Tower, a landmark I always associate with San Francisco, standing tall on the horizon. Down on the street, the traffic was still flowing, and I could see the glow of headlights and taillights, adding to the urban evening atmosphere. The red traffic light on the left also caught my eye, a small pop of color in the fading

I remember seeing these really quirky sculptures at night. They were set up on a small red trailer, kind of like an art installation on wheels. There were three figures, each with a large, lumpy white head, dark bodies, and what looked like white or light-colored bottoms, with one having a light blue waist. The setting was so atmospheric, with a bright full moon shining in the dark sky above, and in the background, a grand, illuminated building with columns added to the dramatic urban feel. It was such an unexpected sight during an evening walk.

I remember looking out the airplane window on this flight, and the view was absolutely breathtaking. Below, there was an endless expanse of snow-capped mountains, stretching out as far as I could see, with some peaks peeking through a layer of haze or clouds. Above it all, the sky was a brilliant, clear blue, a stark contrast to the misty mountain range. Seeing the wingtip in the frame always reminds me of the incredible feeling of being high above the world, on my way to a new adventure.

It wasn't just any bingo; everyone was using corn kernels as markers on these long wooden tables, which made it feel even more like a classic community event. A young man in the foreground is intently looking at his card, ready to mark numbers, and others down the table are just as focused. I remember this moment at the fair, seeing everyone gathered around the 'Fun Wheel' game under one of the big tents. The tent itself had these cool blue and white stripes, giving it that familiar carnival feel, and the grassy ground beneath our feet was a tell-tale sign of being at an outdoor summer event. It was just one of those lively scenes that added to the overall fun atmosphere of the day.

photos.caption: New Idea Farm Equipment - Innovations — New Idea was a farming equipment company founded in Coldwater, OH.

Seeing this really brought to life the history behind some of the brands and equipment often seen at these kinds of community or agricultural fairs. photos.caption: New Idea — My grandfather worked here until he died and raised family of seven on the salary of a factory worker.

These flags always reminded me of some of the older, classic brands you'd see at agricultural fairs or community events, adding to that nostalgic feel of a summer day out.

It was surrounded by a metal fence, ready for the next group of thrill-seekers. You could even spot a bit of another colorful inflatable attraction peeking out on the right. This ride always brought a sense of excitement and wonder, adding to the general buzz of the fair.

The dense green canopies of the trees framed the bottom, and those power lines stretched horizontally across the middle of the frame, adding a touch of urban infrastructure to what felt like a brooding summer sky.
